+ CARLYLE GROUP CARLYLE TO HOST 2026 SHAREHOLDER UPDATE

WASHINGTON and NEW YORK, Feb. 02, 2026 -- Global investment firm Carlyle (NASDAQ: CG) announced today that it will host a Shareholder Update on Thursday, February 26, 2026. The Shareholder Update will include a series of presentations by the company 's senior leadership team and will review Carlyle's growth outlook and financial objectives. Presentations will begin at 8:30 a.m. EST, followed by a Q&A session.

Presentation materials and a live webcast will be posted on the day of the event and can be accessed on the Events & Presentations section of ir.carlyle.com. For those unable to join the live webcast, a replay will be available on the website following the event.

Any questions regarding the 2026 Shareholder Update may be addressed to Carlyles Investor Relations team at publicinvestor@carlyle.com

About Carlyle

Carlyle (NASDAQ: CG) is a global investment firm with deep industry expertise that deploys private capital across three business segments: Global Private Equity, Global Credit, and Carlyle AlpInvest. With $474 billion of assets under management as of September 30, 2025, Carlyles purpose is to invest wisely and create value on behalf of its investors, portfolio companies, and the communities in which we live and invest. Carlyle employs more than 2,400 people in 27 offices across four continents. Further information is available atwww.carlyle.com. Follow Carlyle on X @OneCarlyle and LinkedIn at The Carlyle Group.

+ APOLLO GLOBAL MANAGEMENT APOLLO HYBRID VALUE FUND III RAISES $6.5 BILLION

NEW YORK, May 05, 2026 -- Apollo (NYSE: APO) today announced the final close of Apollo Hybrid Value Fund III (HVF III), raising approximately $6.5 billion in total commitments, reflecting strong support from both new and existing investors. The Fund attracted a diverse, global investor base, including pension funds, sovereign wealth funds, insurance companies, endowments and other institutional and wealth investors.

The Hybrid Value strategy focuses on delivering flexible, partnership-oriented solutions that sit between traditional debt and equity. The strategy primarily invests in structured equity opportunities, including preferred and convertible securities, and provides capital solutions to support growth initiatives, acquisitions, shareholder liquidity and balance sheet optimization, while seeking to provide downside protection and equity participation for investors.

We are grateful for the strong support from both new and existing investors in HVF III, which we believe reflects continued confidence in our strategy and track record, said Jason Scheir, Partner and Head of Hybrid Value at Apollo. We have built the Hybrid Value franchise to deliver bespoke, partnership capital at scale and we remain focused on generating attractive risk-adjusted returns for our investors.

HVF III follows Apollo Hybrid Value Fund I, which closed at $3.3 billion in 2019, and Apollo Hybrid Value Fund II, which closed at $4.6 billion in 2022. Building on this track record, Apollos broader hybrid ecosystem continues to expand with a growing base of capital across its hybrid strategies.

We believe hybrid strategies offer a compelling risk-reward framework for investors as they navigate market cycles and the current period of elevated uncertainty, said Matt Nord, Co-Head of Private Equity and Head of Hybrid at Apollo. Our ability to provide scaled, flexible capital, combined with the strength of our integrated platform, positions us to be the partner of choice for many of the worlds leading companies and sponsors.

Paul, Weiss, Rifkind, Wharton & Garrison LLP represented Apollo in connection with the closing of HVF III.

About Apollo

Apollo is a high-growth, global alternative asset manager. In our asset management business, we seek to provide our clients excess return at every point along the risk-reward spectrum from investment grade credit to private equity. For more than three decades, our investing expertise across our fully integrated platform has served the financial return needs of our clients and provided businesses with innovative capital solutions for growth. Through Athene, our retirement services business, we specialize in helping clients achieve financial security by providing a suite of retirement savings products and acting as a solutions provider to institutions. + APOLLO GLOBAL MANAGEMENT APOLLO CLOSES ACCORD FUND VII AT $1.9 BILLION

Brings Total Capital Raised Across Accord Dislocation Complex to $11.6 Billion Since Inception

NEW YORK, May 04, 2026 -- Apollo (NYSE: APO) today announced the final close of Apollo Accord Fund VII (Accord VII or the Fund) with $1.9 billion in total commitments, reflecting broad support from a global and diverse group of investors including pension funds, financial institutions, endowments, foundations and family offices.

Accord VII is the latest vintage of the Firms flagship Accord Dislocation Series, which has raised $11.6 billion since inception in 2017. The strategy pursues dislocated liquid credit during periods of market volatility, as well as select idiosyncratic and issuer-driven opportunities in more stable market environments. The investment approach emphasizes a diversified portfolio of highly defensible positions targeting the top of the capital structure, across both primary and secondary markets.

We are operating in a period of heightened volatility driven by elevated valuations, increased geopolitical and macroeconomic risk and rapid AI-driven disruption, said Chris Lahoud, Partner and Deputy Co-Head of Hybrid at Apollo. In this environment, markets reward judgment, scale and disciplined underwriting. We believe periods of volatility and dispersion create compelling opportunities for capital providers who are prepared to act decisively.

Akila Grewal, Global Head of the Institutional Client Group at Apollo, added, We are grateful for the continued support of our investors globally. The strong demand for Accord VII reflects sustained confidence in the strategy and the important role it plays within diversified portfolios. In dynamic market environments, we believe the Funds flexible mandate can help investors take advantage of volatility with an emphasis on senior positioning within the capital structure.

Paul, Weiss, Rifkind, Wharton & Garrison LLP represented Apollo in connection with the closing of Accord VII.

About ApolloApollo is a high-growth, global alternative asset manager. In our asset management business, we seek to provide our clients excess return at every point along the risk-reward spectrum from investment grade credit to private equity. For more than three decades, our investing expertise across our fully integrated platform has served the financial return needs of our clients and provided businesses with innovative capital solutions for growth. Through Athene, our retirement services business, we specialize in helping clients achieve financial security by providing a suite of retirement savings products and acting as a solutions provider to institutions. Our patient, creative, and knowledgeable approach to investing aligns our clients, businesses we invest in, our employees, and the communities we impact, to expand opportunity and achieve positive outcomes. + APOLLO FUNDS ACQUIRE GATEHOUSE LIVING GROUP FROM GATEHOUSE BANK

Apollo (NYSE: APO) today announced that Apollo-managed funds have acquired Gatehouse Living Group ("GLG" or "the Group"), a vertically integrated UK residential investment and management platform, from Gatehouse Bank ("GHB"). Financial terms of the transaction were not disclosed.

The Group is comprised of its investment arm, Gatehouse Investment Management ("GIM"), as well as its property management arm, Ascend Properties ("Ascend"). Together, the Group provides institutional investors with a comprehensive suite of operational services to originate and manage new build-to-rent single family housing. As a leader in the UK, the Group has established five platforms and successfully exited three, acquiring more than 5,000 homes from the UK's largest housebuilders. Through Ascend, the Group manages more than 10,000 homes nationwide for both itself and third-party clients, nearly 4,000 of which are operated under the resident-facing white-label Ascend Living brand.

"GLG has led the growth of institutional rental housing, creating a prominent platform that strategically supports the sector and accelerates the delivery of much-needed high-quality housing throughout the UK," said Paul Stockwell, Chief Executive Officer of GLG. "This transaction with Apollo is a testament to the strength of our holistic investment and property management platform and will support our continued expansion in the market."

"GLG, under Gatehouse Bank's stewardship, has established itself as a vertically integrated leader in the UK housing sector, supporting the supply of high-quality, professionally managed rental homes," said Edward Jones, Partner at Apollo. "We look forward to working closely with management to further enhance and expand its operational capabilities and to supporting the platform's continued success as the UK focuses on bolstering homebuilding across tenures and attracting more investment to the sector."

"We are incredibly proud of the success achieved since GLG launched its first investment in 2014 and the critical role we have played, as an early entrant, in establishing the UK Build to Rent market," said Charles Haresnape, Chief Executive Officer of GHB. "The sale is part of Gatehouse Bank's long-term retail growth strategy and will allow for further development of its home finance product offering, which has seen strong demand supporting UK resident, UK expat and international homebuyers and landlords. We are confident that GLG is well positioned today to continuing building on its strong foundation, supporting the supply of new rental homes in the UK."

GLG will continue to be led by CEO Paul Stockwell and its management team, and it will be rebranded with an announcement in due course. The Group will invest its own capital and that of third-party partners, and Ascend will maintain its property management services for external institutional portfolios.

+ QATAR AIRWAYS FURTHER REBUILDS FLIGHT SCHEDULES

Qatar Airways has published its latest revised schedule, reflecting the gradual increase in flights to, and from, Doha to more than 120 destinations by mid-May 2026, across the airline's global network. All flights to, and from, Doha continue to operate through dedicated flight corridors established in close co-ordination with the Qatar Civil Aviation Authority. Please note: Flight schedules are subject to change or cancellation due to operational, regulatory, safety, or other circumstances beyond our control. To view the schedule, click here. Passengers who currently hold a confirmed booking on a flight to the destinations listed in the new schedule will be contacted with new flight information. Please be sure to check the Qatar Airways website or app and ensure your contact details are correct and updated. Source: Company Website

+ 26 MARCH 2026: QATAR AIRWAYS CONTINUES TO RESTORE FLIGHT NETWORK

Qatar Airways has published its latest revised schedule, reflecting the gradual increase in flights to and from Doha. The latest update, valid until 15 April 2026, provides additional frequencies to more than 90 destinations across Qatar Airways' global network. All flights to and from Doha continue to operate through dedicated flight corridors. These routes are established in close coordination with the Qatar Civil Aviation Authority.

Passengers who currently hold a confirmed booking on a flight to one of the destinations listed in the new schedule will be contacted with new flight information. Please be sure to check the Qatar Airways website or app and ensure your contact details are correct and updated. Passengers are kindly asked not to arrive at their departure airport unless they hold a valid, confirmed ticket for travel. Please note: Flight schedules are subject to change or cancellation due to operational, regulatory, safety, or other circumstances beyond our control.

Our teams are doing everything within the current constraints to ensure our passengers reach their destinations. We apologise for any inconvenience caused during this period of disruption and thank passengers for their patience and understanding. Source: Company Website

+ QATAR AIRWAYS TO OPERATE LIMITED RELIEF CORRIDOR FROM DOHA

Qatar Airways scheduled flight operations remain temporarily suspended due to the closure of Qatari airspace. Qatar Airways will resume operations once the Qatar Civil Aviation Authority announces the safe full reopening of Qatari airspace by the relevant authorities. A further update will be provided on 8 March by 09:00 Doha time (06:00 UTC). Following temporary authorisation from the Qatar Civil Aviation Authority confirming a safe operating corridor, Qatar Airways intends to operate repatriation flights on 07 March, departing from Hamad International Airport to the following airports: London (LHR), Paris (CDG), Madrid (MAD), Rome (FCO) and Frankfurt (FRA). Priority on these flights was given to stranded passengers with families, elderly passengers, and those with urgent medical and compassionate travel needs. Each flight was pre-allocated by Qatar Airways directly to such affected passengers. These flights do not constitute a confirmation of resumption of scheduled commercial operations. Passengers are kindly requested not to proceed to the airport unless they have already received an official notification from Qatar Airways for these flights. Qatar Airways has contacted affected passengers directly with their assigned flight details, travel arrangements and next steps. We are working around the clock to organise additional relief flights where operationally possible and will share further updates as soon as they are confirmed. Source: Company Website

DASHBOARD: PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP

PermID4296383614Websitewww.pillsburylaw.com/en/IndustryFull-Service BigLawAddress31 West 52nd Street, New York, NY 10019 USA

ACTIVITIES:

Pillsbury Winthrop Shaw Pittman LLP, founded in 1868, is a global law firm headquartered in New York City. With over 700 lawyers across 20 offices, it serves clients in energy, technology, and finance, specializing in M&A, regulatory, and litigation. Generating over $700 million annually, Pillsbury advised on 2024 renewable energy transactions, recognized by Columbia Law School. Its culture emphasizes innovation, diversity, and pro bono work, with strong associate training programs. Competing with Baker McKenzie, Pillsbury stands out for its energy and tech regulatory expertise. In 2024, it expanded its AI and ESG practices, focusing on sustainable finance. Recent initiatives include cloud-based legal tech and partnerships with nonprofits like Lawyers Alliance for New York, reinforcing its community impact.

+ PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP [1 News That Matters]

+ MORGAN LEWIS STRENGTHENS EMPLOYEE BENEFITS TEAM WITH PILLSBURY TALENT

Morgan Lewis & Bockius LLP has announced the addition of a prominent attorney from Pillsbury Winthrop Shaw Pittman LLP to its Employee Benefits team. This strategic move is seen as a response to the growing complexities in employee benefits law and the increasing demand for expert legal guidance in this area. The attorney, whose experience spans multiple facets of employee benefits, is expected to enhance Morgan Lewis’ capabilities in providing comprehensive legal services to its clients.The announcement comes as Morgan Lewis looks to bolster its offerings in employee benefits law, particularly in the wake of several recent regulatory changes. The attorney's arrival on 08th April 2026 is intended to strengthen the firm's position in an increasingly competitive legal landscape. Morgan Lewis has consistently focused on expanding its practice areas to meet client needs, and this latest addition is part of that ongoing strategy.Pillsbury Winthrop Shaw Pittman, known for its robust practice in employee benefits, has seen a number of its attorneys transition to other firms in recent years. The departing attorney brings a wealth of experience in compliance and regulatory issues, which will be invaluable to Morgan Lewis as it navigates the complexities of employee benefit plans and pension laws. This move not only reflects the attorney's individual career advancement but also highlights the fluid nature of talent within the legal sector.Morgan Lewis has a reputation for its deep expertise in various practice areas, and the inclusion of the Pillsbury attorney is expected to further enhance its service offerings. The firm has been proactive in adapting to changes in legislation affecting employee benefits, ensuring that its clients remain compliant and well-informed. By integrating new talent, Morgan Lewis aims to provide even more specialized services tailored to the evolving needs of its diverse client base.As the legal field continues to evolve, the strategic hiring of top talent from firms like Pillsbury is critical for maintaining a competitive edge. Morgan Lewis’ latest hiring signals its commitment to delivering high-quality legal solutions in employee benefits, reinforcing its reputation as a leading firm in this practice area. The legal community will be watching closely to see how this new addition impacts Morgan Lewis’ capabilities and client service in the months ahead.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ PERKINS COIE [3 News That Matters]

+ ASHURST PERKINS COIE MERGER GAINS STRONG SUPPORT FROM PARTNERS

The merger of global law firms Ashurst and Perkins Coie into the entity Ashurst Perkins Coie has received overwhelming support from the partnerships of both firms. This decision was finalized following a consultation period, with the approval announced on 13th April 2026. The merger is expected to close in the third quarter of the year, contingent upon customary closing conditions.Mark Birnbaum, executive committee chair of Perkins Coie, emphasized the significance of this approval, stating that it reflects the partners' commitment to creating a forward-looking firm. He noted that the merger underlines a shared confidence in their capacity to grow and deliver exceptional legal services in an evolving global market. Paul Jenkins, CEO of Ashurst, also highlighted the alignment between the two firms, asserting that their combined strengths will benefit their clients significantly.The merged firm aims to focus on key sectors including technology, energy, infrastructure, and financial services. Bill Malley, firmwide managing partner at Perkins Coie, expressed optimism that the combination would enhance the firms' abilities to meet clients’ complex cross-border legal needs. The new entity is set to boast approximately 3,000 lawyers across 52 offices in 32 countries, with major hubs located in Seattle, London, Sydney, and New York.The merger, first announced last year, brings together two firms with combined revenues of around USD 2.8 billion. Their lawyers have extensive experience in international markets, having worked in cities such as Brussels, Dubai, Frankfurt, Hong Kong, Paris, Seoul, Shenzhen, Singapore, and Tokyo. The leadership of the new firm will be shared by Malley and Jenkins as global co-CEOs, while Ashurst's global chair, Karen Davies, and Perkins Coie's corporate practice chair, Brian Eiting, will serve as co-chairs.As the legal landscape continues to evolve, the Ashurst Perkins Coie combination is poised to enhance its competitive edge by leveraging the strengths of both firms. This strategic move not only positions the new entity to better serve its clients but also sets the stage for sustained growth and performance in the legal industry.

+ PERKINS COIE AND ASHURST UNITE TO FORM ASHURST PERKINS COIE

NEW YORK — Perkins Coie and Ashurst announced today that the partnerships of both firms have voted overwhelmingly to approve their proposed combination. The approval follows consultation from both firms and reflects strong support for the combination of two law firms with highly complementary geographic reach, sector depth, and practice strength. The date of this announcement is recorded as 2026-04-14 16:18:02.950901.Upon closing, Ashurst Perkins Coie will emerge as a globally integrated firm with combined revenues of approximately US$2.8 billion. The new entity will boast around 3,000 lawyers across more than 50 offices, including flagship hubs in Seattle, London, Sydney, and New York. Ashurst Perkins Coie will be particularly noted for its strengths in the technology, energy and infrastructure, and financial services sectors—industries that are pivotal to the ongoing global economic transformation.Bill Malley, the Firmwide Managing Partner at Perkins Coie, remarked on the significance of this merger by stating, 'Our partnership's approval reflects a shared belief that combining to form Ashurst Perkins Coie will create a truly differentiated global legal platform—one with the scale, sector depth, and technological leadership to meet our clients' increasingly complex, cross-border needs.' This sentiment underscores the strategic vision behind the combination.Paul Jenkins, Global CEO of Ashurst, echoed Malley’s sentiments, emphasizing the strong alignment between the two firms. 'This vote confirms the strong alignment between our firms and our joint ambition for the future,' he stated, highlighting the complementary expertise across sectors and practice areas that will benefit clients. Jenkins added that the merger will create a compelling platform for top legal talent.The combination remains subject to customary closing conditions and is expected to finalize in the third quarter of 2026. As the legal landscape becomes more complex, Ashurst Perkins Coie aims to position itself as a trusted advisor, ready to navigate the intricacies of the global market and deliver exceptional legal services.

+ PERKINS COIE REENTERS CHINA MARKET WITH NEW SHENZHEN OFFICE

Perkins Coie has officially opened a representative office in Shenzhen, marking the firm's significant comeback in the Chinese market. This development comes on the heels of a strategic decision to re-establish an on-the-ground presence in the region, with the office inaugurated on 14th April 2026.The Seattle-based law firm had previously operated offices in Beijing and Shanghai, which were closed in 2024. At that time, Perkins Coie indicated plans to pivot towards Shenzhen, citing the city’s rapid growth as a key tech and manufacturing hub. The firm aims to leverage this new office to enhance its support for Asia-based companies navigating US and cross-border legal needs.Leading the Shenzhen office will be Bing Ai, serving as chief representative and managing partner, along with Wei Yuan, who will act as a representative and litigation partner. Both lawyers are US-trained and bring extensive experience in advising technology-focused clients. Ai specializes in patent procurement and portfolio management in sectors such as semiconductors and medical devices, while Yuan is adept at handling patent infringement disputes.Bill Malley, Perkins Coie’s managing partner, emphasized the firm's commitment to providing US legal expertise across borders. He noted that the opening of the Shenzhen office reflects a dedication to assisting clients in navigating the complexities of global markets, especially in light of the increasing challenges they face.As Perkins Coie re-establishes its presence in mainland China, it stands apart from the trend of office closures and headcount reductions by many leading US firms due to geopolitical tensions and regulatory challenges. However, several international firms have recently opened offices in Shenzhen, attracted by its status within the Greater Bay Area, which has seen substantial economic growth. Perkins Coie’s strategic move comes as the firm prepares for a potential merger with UK-based Ashurst, which would create a top 20 global firm with projected annual revenues of around $2.7 billion.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ COOLEY LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ CROSBY SECURES $60M IN FUNDING, BACKED BY COOLEY LLP

Contract review start-up Crosby has successfully raised $60 million in its latest funding round, marking its third capital raise in less than a year. The funding announcement was made on 3rd April 2026, highlighting the firm’s rapid growth and the interest from prominent investors, including the West Coast-based law firm Cooley LLP.The Series B round was co-led by Lux Capital and Index Ventures, with participation from renowned venture capital firms such as Sequoia, 01 Advisors, and Bain Capital Ventures, as well as angel investor Elad Gil. With this new influx of capital, Crosby plans to enhance its technology capabilities and provide better support to its growing customer base, which includes notable software development firms Clay and Rogo, as well as the financial services operations platform Ramp.Crosby combines artificial intelligence with legal expertise to expedite the contract review process. The firm reported a significant increase in the value of its negotiated contracts, which has soared to just over $1 billion. This is a remarkable rise from the $30 million in brokered contracts when Crosby emerged from stealth mode last year, showcasing the firm’s impressive trajectory in the legal tech space.In a recent blog post, Crosby emphasized its commitment to innovation, stating, 'As we’ve scaled, we’ve also started experimenting with entirely new technology experiences.' The firm has introduced various features, including simulations of counterparty responses to contract redlines, voice agents that negotiate on behalf of clients, and a platform that allows clients to oversee and provide input on Crosby’s work.Founded in 2024 by Ryan Daniels and John Sarihan, Crosby has quickly established itself in the legal tech landscape. Daniels, a former associate at Cooley, has an extensive background in tech entrepreneurship, while Sarihan brings experience as a former Google software engineer and tech lead for Crosby client Ramp. Together, they are steering Crosby towards a future of enhanced contract negotiation and review, supported by substantial investments from Cooley and other leading firms.

+ COOLEY LLP ENHANCES TECH PRACTICE WITH FRESHFIELDS ALUMNI

Cooley LLP has announced a significant expansion of its technology practice, bringing on board several attorneys who previously worked at Freshfields Bruckhaus Deringer. This strategic move, aimed at bolstering Cooley's capabilities in the tech sector, highlights the firm's commitment to remaining competitive in an increasingly dynamic legal landscape.As of 23rd November 2025, Cooley's latest recruitment drive underscores the firm's ongoing efforts to enhance its service offerings in technology, a sector that continues to evolve rapidly. The addition of these high-profile attorneys from Freshfields is expected to not only strengthen Cooley's existing practice but also attract new clients seeking expert legal guidance in technology-related matters.The new team members bring a wealth of experience, having advised prominent clients on a range of legal issues, including intellectual property, data protection, and corporate transactions in the tech industry. Their expertise is anticipated to complement Cooley’s established reputation in venture capital and startup advisory services, further positioning the firm as a leading player in the technology legal sector.Cooley LLP’s decision to recruit from Freshfields reflects a broader trend within the legal industry, where firms are actively seeking to acquire top talent to respond to the demands of a fast-paced, technology-driven market. As legal challenges in the tech sector become more complex, the need for specialized knowledge and skills is paramount, making these strategic hires particularly timely.With this expansion, Cooley LLP aims to solidify its standing as a go-to destination for clients in the technology field, reinforcing its capabilities in an area that is critical for innovation and growth. As the firm integrates its new attorneys, stakeholders will be watching closely to see how these changes impact Cooley's market position and client services in the coming months.

+ COOLEY LLP’S LITIGATION PRACTICE FACES LEADERSHIP CHALLENGES AHEAD

Cooley LLP's litigation practice is making headlines as a finalist for The American Lawyer's General Litigation Department of the Year. With a reputation for excellence, the firm is now navigating through what its partners describe as a 'test of leadership.' This moment comes as Cooley’s litigation team, led by partner Ian Shapiro, seeks to address critical challenges within the evolving legal landscape.The discussion around Cooley's strategic direction took place during a recent interview where Ian Shapiro, along with partners Michael Attanasio and Kathleen Hartnett, shared insights into the firm’s decision-making processes. Notably, they highlighted the firm’s role in representing Jenner & Block in its high-profile bid against the administration, reflecting Cooley's commitment to impactful legal representation. The interview was published just 10 hours ago, providing a timely look into the firm's operations.In addition to its work with Jenner & Block, Cooley has also engaged in various pro bono matters that underscore its commitment to public service and social justice. This dedication is part of what has contributed to the firm's overall growth over the past year. As Cooley LLP continues to expand its litigation department, the firm is poised to leverage its expertise in addressing complex legal challenges while maintaining a strong commitment to its core values.The growth of Cooley LLP's litigation practice is particularly noteworthy as it reflects broader trends within the industry, where firms are increasingly focusing on specialized legal services. This strategic pivot not only enhances Cooley's competitive edge but also positions it favorably for future opportunities. As the firm approaches the end of the fiscal year, stakeholders will undoubtedly be watching Cooley's next moves closely.As this 'test of leadership' unfolds, the legal community and investors alike will be eager to see how Cooley LLP navigates these challenges. The firm's proactive approach, combined with its commitment to excellence, suggests that it may well emerge stronger in an increasingly competitive market.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ REED SMITH LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ REED SMITH STRENGTHENS PRIVATE EQUITY PRACTICE WITH NEW PARTNER IN LA

ReedSmith LLP has announced the addition of a new private equity partner to its Los Angeles office, further solidifying its position in the competitive legal landscape. This strategic move is expected to enhance the firm's offerings in private equity, particularly in a region that has become increasingly vital for investment activities. The firm has been actively expanding its capabilities to address the growing demand from clients in this sector.The newly appointed partner, who joins Reed Smith on 4th May 2026, brings a wealth of experience in private equity transactions and related advisory services. This addition is part of Reed Smith's broader strategy to strengthen its private equity practice and provide comprehensive legal services to its clients, including investment firms and corporate entities involved in mergers and acquisitions.Reed Smith's Los Angeles office is poised to benefit significantly from this new partnership, as the region continues to attract substantial capital inflows into private equity. The firm aims to leverage the partner's expertise to navigate complex transactions and provide tailored solutions that meet the evolving needs of its clients in this fast-paced market. This hire aligns with Reed Smith's commitment to delivering high-quality legal services and fostering long-term relationships with its clients.As the private equity landscape evolves, firms like Reed Smith are recognizing the importance of having experienced professionals who can guide clients through intricate investment structures and regulatory frameworks. The addition of a seasoned partner enhances Reed Smith's ability to offer strategic insights and innovative solutions, reinforcing its reputation as a leading player in the legal sector.With this strategic hire, Reed Smith LLP is well-positioned to capitalize on growth opportunities within the private equity domain. The firm’s commitment to excellence and its proactive approach to meeting client demands will likely contribute to its continued success in a competitive marketplace.

+ REED SMITH LLP APPOINTS NEW LEADER FOR PHILADELPHIA OFFICE

ReedSmith LLP has announced the appointment of a seasoned attorney to lead its Philadelphia office, marking a significant transition for the firm in a key market. The firm has chosen a longtime member of its team, who has demonstrated exceptional legal acumen and leadership capabilities throughout their tenure.The new office leader, whose tenure with Reed Smith spans over a decade, has been a pivotal figure in various high-profile cases and has contributed to the firm’s strategic growth in the region. Effective from 27th April 2026, this appointment signals Reed Smith's commitment to enhancing its local presence and client service in Philadelphia.Reed Smith LLP's decision to appoint an internal candidate reflects its ongoing strategy to promote talent from within while ensuring continuity in leadership. The firm believes that the new office leader's extensive experience and deep understanding of the local legal landscape will not only strengthen client relationships but also inspire the team to pursue innovative legal solutions.The Philadelphia office has been an integral part of Reed Smith LLP’s national network, serving a diverse array of clients across various sectors. The new leader is expected to focus on reinforcing the office's capabilities in key practice areas and expanding its footprint in the competitive legal market.As Reed Smith LLP embarks on this new chapter, stakeholders will be watching closely to see how the fresh leadership will influence the firm’s strategic direction and operational efficiency in Philadelphia. The firm remains optimistic about its prospects under the new guidance and aims to leverage its strong foundation to achieve further growth.

+ REED SMITH APPOINTS FIRST MANAGING PARTNER IN BOSTON

ReedSmith LLP has made a significant addition to its leadership team by appointing its first Managing Partner in Boston. This strategic move aims to enhance the firm's presence in the region, which is becoming increasingly vital in the competitive legal landscape. The appointment comes on the heels of Reed Smith's ongoing efforts to expand its footprint in key markets, and the firm is optimistic about the future under this new leadership.Effective from 23rd April 2026, the newly appointed Managing Partner will spearhead Reed Smith's operations in Boston, focusing on strengthening client relationships and driving business growth. This initiative reflects the firm's commitment to providing top-notch legal services while responding to the evolving needs of its clientele in the Northeast. The choice of a Managing Partner in this pivotal market highlights Reed Smith's strategy to embed itself deeper into the local legal environment.Reed Smith's decision to appoint a Managing Partner in Boston follows an analysis of the region's burgeoning legal market, which has seen increased demand for specialized services across various sectors. With this appointment, Reed Smith aims to leverage local insights and foster partnerships that align with its global vision. The firm is poised to take advantage of Boston's vibrant economy, which is characterized by a mix of established corporations and innovative startups.The new Managing Partner brings a wealth of experience and a robust network of professional relationships that will be instrumental in driving the firm's objectives in Boston. Reed Smith anticipates that this leadership will not only enhance operational efficiencies but also bolster its reputation as a leading legal service provider in the area. As the firm continues to navigate the complexities of the legal market, the Boston office is expected to play a crucial role in its overall growth strategy.In conclusion, Reed Smith's appointment of its first Managing Partner in Boston marks a strategic move to solidify its presence in a key market. urn:contentItem:6JJY-2WG3-RXKX-V11D-00000-00PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP - THE RECENT NEWS OF PERKINS COIE AND PEERS2026-05-13T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:02:04ZLexisNexisSTOCKPILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP - THE RECENT NEWS OF PERKINS COIE AND PEERS

DASHBOARD: PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP

PermID4296383614Websitewww.pillsburylaw.com/en/IndustryFull-Service BigLawAddress31 West 52nd Street, New York, NY 10019 USA

ACTIVITIES:

Pillsbury Winthrop Shaw Pittman LLP, founded in 1868, is a global law firm headquartered in New York City. With over 700 lawyers across 20 offices, it serves clients in energy, technology, and finance, specializing in M&A, regulatory, and litigation. Generating over $700 million annually, Pillsbury advised on 2024 renewable energy transactions, recognized by Columbia Law School. Its culture emphasizes innovation, diversity, and pro bono work, with strong associate training programs. Competing with Baker McKenzie, Pillsbury stands out for its energy and tech regulatory expertise. In 2024, it expanded its AI and ESG practices, focusing on sustainable finance. Recent initiatives include cloud-based legal tech and partnerships with nonprofits like Lawyers Alliance for New York, reinforcing its community impact.

+ PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP [1 News That Matters]

+ MORGAN LEWIS EXPANDS BENEFITS TEAM WITH PILLSBURY HIRE

Morgan Lewis & Bockius LLP has announced the addition of a prominent attorney from Pillsbury Winthrop Shaw Pittman LLP to its Employee Benefits team, a strategic step to address the growing complexities in benefits law and the demand for expert guidance.The attorney's multi-faceted background covers compliance and regulatory issues within employee benefits, and is expected to broaden the firm's capabilities in advising clients on benefit plans and pension laws.Arriving on 08th April 2026, the hire aligns with Morgan Lewis' broader push to strengthen its offerings in employee benefits amid regulatory shifts and an increasingly competitive legal market.Pillsbury has seen several of its attorneys move to other firms in recent years, and Morgan Lewis notes that the new hire will bring important regulatory insight to navigate evolving requirements.The firm has a longstanding reputation for depth in its practice areas, and leaders say the addition will help deliver more specialized services tailored to client needs.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ PERKINS COIE [3 News That Matters]

+ ASHURST-PERKINS COIE MERGER CLEARED BY PARTNERS, CLOSE TARGET SET FOR Q3

Partner votes have backed the Ashurst and Perkins Coie merger, with the approval announced on 13th April 2026 and a closing target in the third quarter, subject to customary conditions.The move underscores a shared commitment to building a forward looking global firm and confidence in growth and in delivering premier legal services in a fast changing market.Paul Jenkins, CEO of Ashurst, said the two firms are well aligned and their combination will broaden client offerings and scale cross border capability.The combined group will number about 3,000 lawyers across 52 offices in 32 countries, with major hubs in Seattle, London, Sydney and New York, and a focus on technology, energy, infrastructure and financial services.The practice areas, together with a revenue base of around USD 2.8 billion, bring extensive international experience spanning Brussels, Dubai, Frankfurt, Hong Kong, Paris, Seoul, Shenzhen, Singapore and Tokyo. Leadership will be shared by Bill Malley and Paul Jenkins as global co-CEOs, with Ashurst global chair Karen Davies and Perkins Coie corporate practice chair Brian Eiting serving as co-chairs.The combination is expected to sharpen the firms' competitive edge and position the new entity for sustained growth in the evolving global legal market.

+ ASHURST AND PERKINS COIE AGREE TO STRATEGIC COMBINATION, LAUNCH ASHURST PERKINS COIE

NEW YORK — Perkins Coie and Ashurst announced today that the partnerships of both firms have voted overwhelmingly to approve their proposed combination, signaling broad support for a deal that pairs complementary geographic reach, sector depth, and practice strength. The date of this announcement is recorded as 2026-04-14 16:18:02.950901.Upon closing, Ashurst Perkins Coie will be a globally integrated firm with combined revenues of approximately US$2.8 billion and around 3,000 lawyers spread across more than 50 offices worldwide. The firm will run flagship hubs in Seattle, London, Sydney, and New York, underscoring its expansive footprint across key markets. It will be well positioned to serve clients in technology, energy and infrastructure, and financial services.Bill Malley, the Firmwide Managing Partner at Perkins Coie, described the vote as recognition that the merger will create a differentiated platform with scale, sector depth, and technology leadership to meet cross-border client needs. 'Our partnership's approval reflects a belief that combining to form Ashurst Perkins Coie will yield a differentiated global platform.'Paul Jenkins, Global CEO of Ashurst, stressed the alignment and ambition behind the move, noting that the combination will unlock complementary strengths across sectors and practice areas that will benefit clients. 'This vote confirms our joint ambition and the compelling platform it creates for top legal talent,' he said.The merger remains subject to customary closing conditions and is expected to finalize in the third quarter of 2026, with the new firm aiming to navigate the evolving global market and deliver enhanced services to clients.

+ PERKINS COIE RE-ESTABLISHES MAINLAND CHINA PRESENCE WITH SHENZHEN HUB

Perkins Coie is re-entering China with a Shenzhen representative office, inaugurated on Apr. 14, 2026, marking a renewed foothold in the market. The move follows the 2024 closure of its Beijing and Shanghai offices and signals a strategic pivot toward Shenzhen to tap the city's rapid growth as a tech and manufacturing hub. The office will be led by Bing Ai, chief representative and managing partner, and Wei Yuan, representative and litigation partner; both are US-trained and bring deep experience serving technology clients. Ai focuses on patent procurement and portfolio management in sectors such as semiconductors and medical devices, while Yuan handles patent infringement disputes. Bill Malley, Perkins Coie's managing partner, says the Shenzhen move underscores the firm's commitment to cross-border US legal expertise and helping clients navigate global markets amid rising cross-border challenges. The development comes as several international firms push deeper into Shenzhen's Greater Bay Area, and Perkins Coie weighs a potential merger with UK-based Ashurst that could create a top-20 global firm with projected annual revenues around $2.7 billion.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ COOLEY LLP [2 News That Matters]

+ CROSBY RAISES $60 MILLION IN SERIES B LED BY LUX CAPITAL AND INDEX VENTURES, WITH COOLEY AMONG BACKERS

Crosby, the contract review start-up, announced it has closed a $60 million Series B on April 3, 2026, its third capital raise in under a year, with Cooley LLP among the backers.The round was co-led by Lux Capital and Index Ventures, with participation from Sequoia, 01 Advisors, Bain Capital Ventures and angel investor Elad Gil.The fresh capital will fund product development and help Crosby scale to serve a growing roster of clients, including Clay, Rogo and Ramp.Crosby uses artificial intelligence paired with legal expertise to accelerate contract reviews, and the company says the value of negotiated contracts now tops $1 billion, up from roughly $30 million when it emerged from stealth last year.In a blog post, Crosby stated, "As we’ve scaled, we’ve also started experimenting with entirely new technology experiences." The company has rolled out simulations of counterparty responses to contract redlines, voice agents that negotiate on clients’ behalf, and a client-facing platform to oversee Crosby’s work.Crosby was founded in 2024 by Ryan Daniels and John Sarihan. Daniels, a former Cooley associate, and Sarihan, a former Google software engineer and Ramp tech lead, have steered Crosby toward a future of faster contract negotiation with the support of investors including Cooley and other leading firms.

+ COOLEY TAPS FRESHFIELDS BRUCKHAUS DERINGER ALUMNI TO BOOST TECH PRACTICE

Cooley LLP is expanding its technology practice by recruiting several attorneys who previously worked at Freshfields Bruckhaus Deringer, signaling a strategic effort to strengthen its tech capabilities and stay competitive in a dynamic legal market.As of 23rd November 2025, the firm’s latest recruitment drive highlights ongoing work to broaden technology offerings in a rapidly evolving sector. The addition of Freshfields Bruckhaus Deringer alumni is anticipated to bolster the existing practice and help attract clients seeking specialized tech law guidance.The incoming team members bring broad experience, advising notable clients on IP, data protection, and corporate tech transactions. Their know-how is expected to augment Cooley’s well-established venture capital and startup advisory practice, further positioning the firm as a leading tech law player.The hire reflects a broader market trend toward poaching top talent to meet demands of a fast-paced, tech-driven environment. With tech-related legal challenges growing more complex, specialized knowledge is increasingly vital, making these hires timely.Through this expansion, Cooley seeks to reinforce its standing as a go-to tech law firm, supporting its innovation-driven growth. As integration proceeds, stakeholders will watch how these changes affect Cooley's market position and client services in the near term.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ REED SMITH LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ REED SMITH EXPANDS LA PRIVATE EQUITY TEAM WITH SENIOR PARTNER APPOINTMENT

ReedSmith LLP has expanded its private equity practice with the appointment of a new partner in its Los Angeles office, a move aimed at strengthening service to investment firms and corporate clients in mergers and acquisitions.The partner joins on 4th May 2026 and brings substantial experience in private equity transactions and related advisory services, supporting the firm's effort to navigate complex deals.The Los Angeles market continues to attract capital inflows into private equity, and the firm expects to leverage the new partner's expertise to tailor solutions for its clients.The addition reinforces Reed Smith's reputation for high-quality legal services and long-term client relationships as the private equity landscape evolves and demands more sophisticated guidance on investment structures and regulatory matters.The firm is well-positioned to capitalize on growth opportunities in private equity with this hire.

+ REED SMITH APPOINTS LONGTIME PHILADELPHIA EXECUTIVE TO LEAD LOCAL PRACTICE

ReedSmith LLP has named an internal partner to lead its Philadelphia office, underscoring a continuity-driven transition in a key market. The appointee has spent more than ten years at the firm and has played a pivotal role in high-profile cases, helping shape strategy for the Philadelphia footprint. The decision to promote from within aligns with the firm's strategy to cultivate internal talent while ensuring leadership continuity. The Philadelphia office is a core part of Reed Smith's national network, serving clients across sectors, and the new leader will prioritize strengthening core practice areas and expanding the firm's local footprint. Effective from 27th April 2026, the move signals optimism for future growth as the firm seeks to deepen client relationships and drive operational excellence in Philadelphia.

+ REED SMITH NAMES BOSTON THE SITE OF ITS INAUGURAL MANAGING PARTNER ROLE, SIGNALING GROWTH

ReedSmith LLP has appointed its first Managing Partner in Boston, signaling a new era for the firm in the Northeast market. Effective from 23rd April 2026, the appointment designates a local leader to head Boston operations and advance client development. The role is designed to strengthen relationships with current clients while pursuing new business opportunities across sectors where Boston has shown momentum. The firm noted that the decision follows an analysis of the region's burgeoning legal market and Reed Smith's broader expansion strategy in key markets. By tapping into local insights and forming partnerships aligned with its global vision, Reed Smith aims to embed itself more deeply into Boston's business landscape. The new Managing Partner brings a wealth of experience and a wide professional network that executives expect will raise operational efficiency and bolster the firm's reputation in the area. The Boston office is set to be a central pillar of the firm's growth plan as Reed Smith navigates the evolving legal market, with investors and stakeholders watching closely in the coming months.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ WILSON SONSINI GOODRICH & ROSATI P.C. [3 News That Matters]

+ BIOORBIT RAISES $13.2 MILLION SEED FINANCING WITH WILSON SONSINI AS COUNSEL

BioOrbit has closed a seed financing of $13.2 million (£9.8 million), with counsel from Wilson Sonsini Goodrich & Rosati P.C. The round was co-led by LocalGlobe and BREEGA, with participation from Seedcamp, Auxxo Female Catalyst Fund, Type One Ventures, 7percent Ventures and other investors.The funding was announced on 30th April 2026.BioOrbit's leadership includes Cambridge scientist Dr. Katie King and oncology researcher Dr. Leonor Teles, who are pursuing a space-based pharmaceuticals factory within the next decade. The company is developing hardware to enable large-scale crystallization of protein drugs, an essential step in biopharmaceutical manufacturing.Wilson Sonsini's team, led by Daniel Glazer and supported by Jose Campos, Rashad Mohammed and Sophia Kunthara, provided corporate and emerging-company counsel to BioOrbit during the financing, positioning the startup for its ambitious space-manufacturing plans.The investment highlights growing interest in life sciences innovations for space applications, a trend the firm says is drawing increasing attention from investors. BioOrbit also directs stakeholders to LinkedIn for updates and to UKTN for additional coverage.

+ OFF PISTE CAPITAL CLOSES FUND I WITH WILSON SONSINI COUNSEL, SIGNALS INFRASTRUCTURE-FOCUSED VC PATH

Wilson Sonsini Goodrich & Rosati P.C. said it advised Off Piste Capital on the launch of its first fund, Off Piste Fund I. The new venture capital firm, focused on backing innovative founders, closed the fund on 29th April 2026 with a select group of strategic limited partners, including Edition Ventures.Off Piste Fund I aims to invest in companies building the systems and infrastructure for the next era. The fund has already deployed capital into four startups: Stendr, a Norway-based maker of AI-driven command and control tech for counter-drone operations; Objection, a UK firm building accountability infrastructure for public information; Beyond Reach Labs, a US YC-backed outfit creating deployable space structures; and Octet, a UK company developing cryptographic proofs of physical location.Legal work was led by Wilson Sonsini partners and associates including Bradley Doline, Alice Cao, and Stephanie Yasay, who guided fund formation, regulatory considerations, and closing mechanics. The cooperation highlights Wilson Sonsini's active role in supporting emerging venture activity and its growing position in the technology and infrastructure investment ecosystem.With Fund I now active Off Piste plans follow-on investments across sectors where next-generation systems and infrastructure enable growth. The partnership with Wilson Sonsini and the presence of Edition Ventures as a backer underscore a broader industry interest in technology-enabled infrastructure.Interested observers can view Off Piste's founder's LinkedIn post or EU-Startups coverage for more context about the launch and strategy.

+ KEN SANOCKI JOINS WILSON SONSINI AS ECS PARTNER, BOOSTING ENERGY-TRANSITION FINANCING

Wilson Sonsini Goodrich & Rosati named Ken Sanocki a partner in its Energy and Climate Solutions practice, expanding the firm's capacity to guide clients through complex energy-infrastructure financing during the energy transition.Sanocki previously served as vice president of legal at Sunrun, the largest residential solar and storage provider in the United States, where he oversaw all project-finance matters, including tax equity financings and debt structures for large-scale distributed-energy projects. Since 5th May 2026, he has led more than 50 tax equity financings totaling over $10 billion across diverse distributed-energy portfolios.Doug Clark, the firm's managing partner, commented, "Ken has operated at the center of the energy transition, helping shape the financing structures that have enabled distributed energy to scale into mainstream infrastructure." His expertise in tax equity and structured financings, paired with commercial instincts, will help clients navigate energy-financing complexities.At Wilson Sonsini, Sanocki will advise companies, sponsors, and investors on structured and project finance across renewable energy, storage, and climate infrastructure, and support capital formation and strategic transactions, including asset-portfolio development and preparation for IPOs or M&A. The firm has been expanding its clean-energy advisory capabilities through partner additions who specialize in structured finance and regulatory matters.Sanocki's background also includes serving as senior counsel for global structured and project finance at SunEdison and beginning his career as a project-finance associate at Milbank. He earned a J.D. from NYU School of Law and a B.A. from Boston College. His arrival, alongside other recent partner hires, signals Wilson Sonsini's strengthened position in sophisticated energy-transition finance.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ SIDLEY AUSTIN LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ TELIX-REGENERON RADIOPHARMACEUTICAL PACT VALUED UP TO USD 2.1 BILLION; SIDLEY AUSTIN ADVISES TELIX

Sidley Austin is advising Telix Pharmaceuticals on its strategic collaboration with Regeneron Pharmaceuticals, a partnership valued at up to USD 2.1 billion. The arrangement will see Telix and Regeneron co-developing and co-commercialising next-generation radiopharmaceutical therapies under a 50:50 cost-and-profit-sharing model, with Telix receiving USD 40 million upfront for four initial programmes. Disclosed on 21st April 2026, the deal centers on joint governance and shared development and commercialization responsibilities, aiming to leverage both companies' oncology platforms. Telix is pursuing an aggressive expansion in radiopharmaceuticals, seeking to broaden its footprint in cancer care through partnerships that combine Regeneron's oncology capabilities with Telix's radiopharmaceutical pipeline. Joshua Hofheimer, a Sidley partner, commented that "These considerations affect governance, regulatory matters, and intellectual property, in particular." The alliance is positioned to accelerate radiopharmaceutical development while balancing regulatory and commercial considerations. Industry observers note that the deal aligns with a broader wave of M&A activity in the biopharma space, and Sidley Austin continues to build its reputation as a premier adviser on cross-border collaborations in a changing regulatory environment.

+ SIDLEY AUSTIN EXPANDS CITY FOOTPRINT AS REVENUE RISES 33% ON HIRING DRIVE

Sidley Austin is reporting a 33% increase in City revenue, credited to a targeted hiring push as of 14th April 2026. The growth underscores Sidley's expanded City footprint following recruitment of senior lawyers and other specialists. Executives say the hires have diversified practice areas and improved client service, lifting billable hours and retention. Leadership intends to refine hiring practices and broaden capabilities to stay ahead in a competitive market. Industry observers see the firm's approach as a potential model for other firms navigating tighter margins and evolving client needs.

+ CFTC TAPS SIDLEY AND LATHAM TO SHAPE FORWARD-LOOKING RULES FOR DIGITAL DERIVATIVES

The CFTC has established an Innovation Task Force by engaging SidleyAustin and Latham & Watkins to advance the regulatory framework for the derivatives markets, aligning oversight with rapid technological change.The agency announced on 11th April 2026 that Sidley Austin, with deep regulatory experience, and Latham & Watkins, providing strategic counsel, will help translate market developments into robust policy.Sidley Austin's team is expected to offer guidance on best practices as the derivatives space integrates technologies such as blockchain and artificial intelligence.The task force forms part of a broader CFTC strategy to both regulate and facilitate innovation, seeking to balance risk management with opportunities for market participants to deploy new tools.Industry stakeholders will watch for potential policy shifts and how the collaboration could influence regulatory expectations, with implications for U.S. competitiveness in global markets.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ GREENBERG TRAURIG, LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ GREENBERG TRAURIG COUNSELS GOLDEN ENTERTAINMENT IN PRIVATIZATION BY FAMILY STAKEHOLDERS

Greenberg Traurig, LLP represented Blake L. Sartini in the successful transition of Golden Entertainment to a private, family-owned operation, with closing finalized on 30th April 2026. The move marks a milestone for Sartini, a respected figure in the gaming industry.As part of the deal, shareholders received a cash dividend of $2.75 and 0.902 shares of VICI Properties Inc. for each Golden share, a structure designed to provide immediate liquidity while preserving exposure to a major gaming and hospitality platform.VICI Properties acquired the real estate assets tied to seven Golden Entertainment casino resorts, and a triple-net master lease was established with Sartini-controlled entities, allowing Golden to continue operating its casino and tavern portfolio without interruption.Co-Managing Shareholder Michael J. Bonner of Greenberg Traurig's Las Vegas office commented, "We were honored to represent Blake L. Sartini in returning Golden Entertainment to private family ownership. We congratulate Blake and the entire Golden Entertainment team!" The firm highlighted its proficiency in corporate transactions and real estate, noting the collaboration of a team including Dmitriy A. Tartakovskiy, Jim Mace, and James Doyle, among others.With about 3,100 lawyers across 51 locations globally, Greenberg Traurig continues to be recognized for its innovative legal services and strategic solutions in the corporate and real estate space.

+ TOKYO OFFICE HOSTS ANREV BRIEFING ON REAL ASSETS, DATA CENTRES AND AI OPPORTUNITIES

Greenberg Traurig, LLP's Tokyo office hosted a briefing for ANREV on Real Assets & New Technologies: Data Centres & AI Opportunities on the 30th of April, 2026. The panel included Greenberg Traurig Shareholder Joel H. Rothstein, who chairs the Asia Real Estate Practice, as well as other experts. The session explored the transformative potential of artificial intelligence for capital allocation, infrastructure planning, and investment strategies across global real asset markets, with particular emphasis on the surge in data centre activity in the Asia-Pacific region driven by rapid digitalization. Rothstein highlighted Greenberg Traurig's commitment to fostering industry dialogue about trends shaping real estate and infrastructure. He stated, 'These conversations are increasingly important as investors and developers navigate the rapid convergence of digital infrastructure, real estate, and emerging technologies.' The firm leverages its cross-border capabilities to support complex, multi-jurisdictional investments in digital infrastructure and other real assets. Greenberg Traurig, with roughly 3,100 lawyers across 51 locations, is known for its innovative approach to real estate and infrastructure law. The firm has earned recognition as a 2025 BTI Best of the Best Recommended Law Firm and consistently appears on Am Law Global 100 and Law360 400 lists.

+ MELTONES DELIVER VICTORY FOR CHARITABLE CAUSES AT LAW ROCKS LOS ANGELES

On 29th April 2026, Greenberg Traurig, LLP's house band The Meltones emerged victorious at Law Rocks Los Angeles, staged at Whisky A Go Go in West Hollywood, as donations and sponsorships topped the night’s haul to exceed $65,000, with $41,000 designated for The Headstrong Project.The Law Rocks event, spanning three nights, set a regional record by raising more than $569,000—the most for Los Angeles in 15 years. The Meltones opened with a Green Day anthem, and the outcome was decided by a mix of fan contributions, ticket sales, and judges’ scores. Greenberg Traurig also served as a Hall of Fame sponsor.The Meltones’ roster blends partners and staff across real estate, government policy, and litigation, including Stephen L. Rabinowitz and Burt Bruton on guitar, Christopher A. Cernik on keyboard, and vocalists Michael R. Hogue, Ashley A. LeBlanc, and Tracy Newirth; guest Tom Helf (drums) and Daniel Blumberg (bass) complemented the lineup. The group, named for co-founder Mel Greenberg, has long been a fixture of GT’s culture and has continued to spotlight the firm’s community efforts, including a recent Law Rocks NYC win raising over $100,000 for The Door. Greenberg Traurig now operates with about 3,100 lawyers in 51 offices worldwide.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ GOODWIN PROCTER LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ SIDLEY AND GOODWIN STEER ESPERION'S $1.1B TAKE-PRIVATE IN BIOTECH SECTOR SHIFT

Esperion Therapeutics Inc. has entered a $1.1 billion take-private agreement, with Sidley Austin LLP and Goodwin Procter LLP handling the transaction's legal work. The deal, announced on 1st May 2026, is designed to reposition the lipid-management specialist away from public market volatility and toward a deeper focus on its programs.The arrangement comes amid growing investor and private equity interest in biotechnology, with private ownership seen as a way to streamline operations and accelerate development.Sidley and Goodwin are recognized for their mergers-and-acquisitions expertise and have been tasked with guiding the process through governance and regulatory considerations.Industry analysts say moving to private ownership could enable longer-term planning and increased investment in R&D, with investor attention turning to Esperion's performance after the transition.The deal also spotlights the pivotal role of major law firms in facilitating large biotech transactions, potentially signaling more private-market activity in the sector.

+ FBT GIBBONS ADDS VENTURE CAPITAL ATTORNEY FROM GOODWIN PROCTER LLP

On 21st April 2026, FBT Gibbons confirmed the recruitment of a venture capital attorney from Goodwin Procter LLP to its New Jersey office. The hire strengthens the firm's venture capital practice as the sector grows. The incoming attorney brings broad experience from Goodwin Procter LLP, known for its robust venture capital work spanning startups and established companies. FBT Gibbons says the addition will help clients raise capital and navigate complex investment environments. The move reflects a wider industry trend of firms expanding niche capabilities to serve clients in New Jersey and beyond.

+ TOPSPIN CONSUMER PARTNERS CLOSES $328 MILLION FUND WITH LEGAL SUPPORT FROM GOODWIN PROCTER

Goodwin Procter LLP supported Topspin Consumer Partners in closing its latest fundraising effort, securing $328 million for a consumer-focused fund. The achievement marks a notable development in the private equity landscape, with Goodwin Procter providing essential counsel throughout the process.On April 15, 2026, Topspin announced the fund’s close, signaling an intent to capitalize on opportunities within the consumer sector. The firm’s legal team helped navigate complex fundraising dynamics, ensure regulatory compliance, and streamline commitments from potential investors.The fund will pursue a wide range of opportunities across consumer markets, with a focus on sectors showing robust growth. Through Goodwin Procter’s support, Topspin Consumer Partners seeks to identify and invest in companies well-positioned to perform in a changing market environment.The collaboration highlights Goodwin Procter’s reputation in private equity and its ongoing commitment to helping clients achieve their investment objectives, potentially boosting investor confidence as the fund scales.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ ORRICK, HERRINGTON & SUTCLIFFE LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ ORRICK ADDS EXPERIENCED USPTO VETERAN TO EXPAND IP PRACTICE

Orrick, Herrington & Sutcliffe has expanded its IP practice by hiring a senior attorney from the USPTO, a move that reflects the firm's ongoing push into technology and telecommunications sectors.The new attorney will officially join on the 28th of April, 2026, bringing a wealth of experience from the USPTO, including patent examination and litigation.The hire is expected to augment Orrick's ability to guide clients through patent strategy and protection in rapidly changing markets.Orrick's managing partner commented that the addition reinforces the firm's commitment to best-in-class IP services, with professionals who understand patent law's intricacies.Industry observers note that the firm's ability to attract talent from leading institutions could influence its growth trajectory and competitive position in technology-driven sectors.

+ NICOLE LOPEZ JOINS ORRICK TO DRIVE YOUTH ONLINE SAFETY STRATEGY AND REGULATION

Orrick, Herrington & Sutcliffe announced the addition of Nicole Lopez, a recognized authority in youth online safety policy and litigation, to its team. Lopez previously held a senior role at Meta, underscoring her capability to navigate complex safety issues as the digital environment evolves.On Apr. 28, 2026, Lopez expressed enthusiasm for the new role and the opportunity to broaden her impact in the field.Her background spans policy, litigation and regulatory strategy, which the firm says will help clients facing increasing scrutiny over online safety. Jeremy Kudon, who leads Orrick's State Legislative practice, said, "We are facing a watershed moment. Online safety is top of mind for innovators, government, teens and parents, and society at large." The firm notes that 25 state legislatures are actively considering legislation aimed at safeguarding minors in digital spaces.Aravind Swaminathan, Orrick's Tech Sector Co-Leader, called Lopez a unique blend of skills—"part policy expert, part litigation and regulatory strategist, and part business advisor"—and said she will be an extraordinary resource for clients as AI reshapes the digital landscape. The Online Safety Law Center will leverage her leadership to keep clients informed of rapid developments and to advocate for policies that balance innovation with child protection.

+ ORRICK BOLSTERS DIGITAL SAFETY TEAM WITH META VETERAN

On Apr. 23, 2026, Orrick, Herrington & Sutcliffe announced the appointment of the former Meta youth safety policy head, adding depth to its digital security and regulatory practice.The incoming advisor brings a robust background in policy formulation and implementation, with specific focus on safeguarding young users in digital environments.Officials say the hire will help Orrick advise clients on a broad set of issues, including privacy protections, data privacy regulations, and compliance obligations tied to online youth safety.The recruitment reflects Orrick's broader strategy to stay ahead of regulatory developments and position itself as a leading adviser on technology-related risk for a changing legal landscape.Industry observers expect the appointment to enhance the firm's ability to help clients navigate complex rules as platforms face increasing scrutiny over practices affecting minors.

urn:contentItem:6JK1-KM23-RS4K-530W-00000-00FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC - KEEPING AN EYE ON THE SECTOR: INDUSTRY & PEERS UPDATE2026-05-13T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:02:04ZLexisNexisSTOCKFORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC - KEEPING AN EYE ON THE SECTOR: INDUSTRY & PEERS UPDATE

DASHBOARD: FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC

PermID4295901538Websitehttps://www.fortress.com/IndustryAlternative InvestmentsAddress1345 Avenue OF The Americas Florida 46 New York, NY, 10105-4302 United States

ACTIVITIES:

Fortress Investment Group LLC, founded in 1998, is a global investment firm headquartered in New York City. Employing over 900 people, it manages $48 billion in assets across private equity, credit, and real estate. Acquired by SoftBank in 2017, Fortress operates independently, focusing on distressed assets and infrastructure. In 2024, it invested $2 billion in renewable energy projects. Its culture emphasizes risk management, innovation, and agility, with training in asset valuation and fintech. Competing with Apollo Global, Fortress stands out for its credit expertise and opportunistic investments. Recent initiatives include blockchain-based real estate funds and ESG-focused portfolios, aligning with investment trends. Fortress diversified strategy ensures resilience in volatile markets.

+ FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC [3 Press Releases]

+ FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC MAMMOTH FREIGHTERS RECEIVES FAA CERTIFICATION FOR 777-200LRMF FREIGHTER

Fort Worth, Texas - April 8, 2026 - Mammoth Freighters LLC today announced it has received certification from the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) for its 777-200LRMF (Long Range Mammoth Freighter), marking a major milestone in the company's expansion of next-generation widebody freighter solutions.

FAA certification validates the aircraft's design, engineering, and performance, clearing the 777-200LRMF for commercial service. The platform delivers a compelling combination of long-range capability, payload efficiency, and operational reliability, positioning it as a highly versatile solution for global cargo networks.

Source: Company Website

+ BROOKFIELD ASSET MANAGEMENT LTD [3 Press Releases]

+ BROOKFIELD ASSET MANAGEMENT ANNOUNCES PRICING OF $550 MILLION OF NOTES DUE 2031 AND $450 MILLION RE-OPENING OF NOTES DUE 2036

NEW YORK, April 14, 2026 -- Brookfield Asset Management Ltd. (BAM) (NYSE: BAM, TSX: BAM), a leading global alternative asset manager headquartered in New York with over $1 trillion of assets under management, today priced an offering of (i) $550 million principal amount of senior notes due 2031, which will bear interest at a rate of 4.832% per annum (the 2031 notes), and (ii) $450 million re-opening of its 5.298% notes due 2036 (the new 2036 notes and, together with the 2031 notes, the notes).

The new 2036 notes will form part of the same series as the already outstanding $400 million principal amount of 5.298% notes due 2036 (the existing 2036 notes), which were issued on November 18, 2025. After giving effect to the re-opening, the aggregate principal amount of the series will be $850 million. The terms of the new 2036 notes will be identical to the existing 2036 notes, other than the issue date and the issue price. The new 2036 notes will be issued at a price equal to 98.962% of their principal value plus accrued and unpaid interest from November 18, 2025 (the issue date of the existing 2036 notes) through, but excluding, the date of delivery of such notes, with an effective yield of 5.434%, if held to maturity.

The net proceeds from the sale of the notes will be used for general corporate purposes. The offering is expected to close on April 17, 2026, subject to the satisfaction of customary closing conditions.

The notes are being offered pursuant to prospectus supplements to BAMs existing base shelf prospectus filed in the United States and Canada and pursuant to an effective registration statement on Form F-10 on file with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission.

Copies of the prospectus supplement and accompanying base shelf prospectus may be obtained free of charge on EDGAR at www.sec.gov/edgar or on SEDAR+ at www.sedarplus.ca. + BROOKFIELD ASSET MANAGEMENT ANNOUNCES $1 BILLION COMMERCIAL PAPER PROGRAM

NEW YORK, March 03, 2026 -- Brookfield Asset Management Ltd. (NYSE: BAM) (TSX: BAM) (Brookfield) announced today that it has established a commercial paper program on a private placement basis. Under the program, Brookfield may issue unsecured commercial paper notes (the notes) up to a maximum aggregate amount outstanding at any time of $1 billion. The program is expected to further diversify and strengthen Brookfields balance sheet by providing another source of short-term capital. The proceeds from the issuance of any notes will be used for general corporate purposes.

The notes to be offered under the program have not been and will not be registered under the Securities Act of 1933, as amended (the Securities Act), and may not be offered or sold in the United States absent registration or an applicable exemption from registration requirements of the Securities Act and applicable state laws. This press release shall not constitute an offer to sell or the solicitation of an offer to buy notes under Brookfield Asset Managements commercial paper program and is being issued in accordance with Rule 135c under the Securities Act.

About Brookfield Asset Management

Brookfield Asset Management Ltd. (NYSE: BAM, TSX: BAM) is a leading global alternative asset manager, headquartered in New York, with over $1 trillion of assets under management across renewable power and transition, infrastructure, private equity, real estate, and credit. We offer a range of alternative investment products to investors around the world including public and private pension plans, endowments and foundations, sovereign wealth funds, financial institutions, insurance companies and private wealth investors. + BROOKFIELD ASSET MANAGEMENT ANNOUNCES RENEWAL OF NORMAL COURSE ISSUER BID

NEW YORK, Jan. 09, 2026 -- Brookfield Asset Management Ltd. (NYSE: BAM, TSX: BAM) (Brookfield Asset Management) today announced it has received the requisite approval for the renewal of its normal course issuer bid providing the option to purchase up to 36,946,177 Class A Limited Voting Shares (Class A Shares), representing approximately 10% of the public float of Brookfield Asset Managements outstanding Class A Shares. Purchases under the bid will be made on the open market through the facilities of the New York Stock Exchange (NYSE), Toronto Stock Exchange (TSX), and/or alternative trading systems. The period of the normal course issuer bid will extend from January 13, 2026 to January 12, 2027, or an earlier date should Brookfield Asset Management complete its purchases. Brookfield Asset Management will pay the market price at the time of acquisition for any Class A Shares purchased or such other price as may be permitted.

As at December 31, 2025, the number of Class A Shares issued and outstanding totaled 1,637,941,906 of which 369,461,770 shares represented the public float. The maximum daily purchase on the TSX under this bid will be 365,499 Class A Shares, which is 25% of 1,461,999 (the average daily trading volume for Class A Shares on the TSX for the six months ended December 31, 2025).

Of the 37,123,295 Class A Shares approved for purchase under Brookfield Asset Managements prior normal course issuer bid that commenced on January 13, 2025 and will expire on January 12, 2026, Brookfield Asset Management purchased 6,548,561 Class A Shares as of December 31, 2025; 100,000 Class A Shares through open market purchases on the TSX and 6,448,561 Class A Shares through open market purchases on the NYSE and/or alternative trading systems. The weighted average price that Brookfield Asset Management paid per Class A Share acquired under this bid was US$54.14.

Brookfield Asset Management is renewing its normal course issuer bid to retain the option to acquire its Class A Shares where this aligns with its investment and capital allocation strategies. All Class A Shares acquired by Brookfield Asset Management under this bid will be cancelled and/or purchased by a non-independent trustee pursuant to the terms of Brookfield Asset Managements long-term incentive plans.

Brookfield Asset Management also announced that it has entered into an automatic purchase plan in relation to the normal course issuer bid. The automatic purchase plan allows for the potential purchase of Class A Shares during the term of the normal course issuer bid, subject to certain trading parameters, at times when Brookfield Asset Management ordinarily would not be active in the market due to its own internal trading black-out period, insider trading rules or otherwise. Outside of these periods, Class A Shares will be repurchased in accordance with managements discretion and in compliance with applicable law.
