+ CARLYLE GROUP L.P. [3 Press Releases]

+ CARLYLE GROUP CENTERRA GOLD ANNOUNCES EXECUTIVE CHANGES

TORONTO, March 30, 2026 -- Centerra Gold Inc. (Centerra or the Company) (TSX: CG) (NYSE: CGAU) announces that David Hendriks, Executive Vice President and Chief Operating Officer (COO), will be leaving the Company. He will remain available to the Company in a consulting capacity to support a smooth transition. Mike Sylvestre will assume the responsibilities of COO on an interim basis, effective today.

President and CEO, Paul Tomory, commented, As we begin the search for a permanent replacement, we are pleased to welcome Mike Sylvestre as our interim Chief Operating Officer. Mike brings decades of operational leadership and deep technical expertise, and we are confident his experience and results-driven approach will support our sites as we remain focused on execution across our operating assets and continue to develop our organic growth pipeline of projects.

Mr. Sylvestre is a seasoned mining executive with over 45 years of international experience and has held leadership roles at major, mid-tier and junior mining companies. He most recently served as Senior Vice President, Americas at Kinross Gold, retiring in 2022. Over the course of his career, he has held a range of senior operational and executive roles, with a strong track record in leading both established and start-up operations, driving safety performance, operational excellence, and sustainable practices. He currently serves on the boards of Hochschild Mining and Vista Gold Corp. Mr. Sylvestre holds a Master of Science in Mining and Mineral Engineering from McGill University and a Bachelor of Science in Mining Engineering from Queens University and is a member of the Professional Engineers of Ontario.

About Centerra Gold

Centerra Gold Inc. is a Canadian-based gold mining company focused on operating, developing, exploring and acquiring gold and copper properties in North America, Turkiye, and other markets worldwide. Centerra operates two mines: the Mount Milligan Mine in British Columbia, Canada, and the Oksut Mine in Turkiye. The Company also owns the Kemess Project in British Columbia, Canada, the Goldfield Project in Nevada, United States, and owns and operates the Molybdenum Business Unit in the United States and Canada. Centerras shares trade on the Toronto Stock Exchange (TSX) under the symbol CG and on the New York Stock Exchange (NYSE) under the symbol CGAU. The Company is based in Toronto, Ontario, Canada.

For more information:

+ APOLLO GLOBAL MANAGEMENT APOLLO FUNDS ACQUIRE MAJORITY STAKE IN NOBLE ENVIRONMENTAL, INC.

Investment Supports Vertically Integrated Regional Waste Management Platform Serving the Northeast, Mid-Atlantic and MidwestUnited States

NEW YORK, May 12, 2026 -- Apollo (NYSE: APO) today announced that Apollo-managed funds (Apollo Funds) have acquired a majority interest in Noble Environmental, Inc. (Noble Environmental or the Company), a vertically integrated waste management platform headquartered in Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania.

Founded in 2016, Noble Environmental is a regional leader in waste management across the Northeast, Mid-Atlantic and MidwestUnited States, providing integrated services spanning solid waste collection, hauling, transfer and disposal to municipalities and commercial customers, alongside a growing RNG business that captures and converts landfill gas into pipeline-quality fuel. The Companys portfolio of landfills, transfer stations and hauling operations represent long-life hard assets that provide essential services to the communities served by Noble Environmental.

Scott Browning and Brad Fierstein, Partners at Apollo, said, Noble Environmental has built a differentiated platform in one of the most attractive waste markets in the country. The combination of scarce, permitted landfill assets, a fully integrated service offering and a growing RNG business creates a compelling foundation for long-term value creation. Apollo has a long history of investing in essential service businesses, and we look forward to partnering with the Companys management team to expand Noble Environmentals platform and build on its strong foundation.

David Florance, President and Chief Operating Officer of Noble Environmental, said, Since inception, Noble Environmentals goal has been to build a leading waste management business that delivers for the communities we serve while creating lasting value through disciplined operations and strategic growth. Apollo shares that vision, and their experience and resources will be invaluable as we continue to execute on our vision to grow the business.

Latham & Watkins LLP served as legal counsel to the Apollo Funds.

Guggenheim Securities, LLC served as financial advisor to the Strategic Alternatives Committee of the Board of Directors of Noble Environmental (the Noble Strategic Alternatives Committee), and Vinson & Elkins LLP served as legal counsel to the Noble Strategic Alternatives Committee.

+ APOLLO GLOBAL MANAGEMENT APOLLO HYBRID VALUE FUND III RAISES $6.5 BILLION

NEW YORK, May 05, 2026 -- Apollo (NYSE: APO) today announced the final close of Apollo Hybrid Value Fund III (HVF III), raising approximately $6.5 billion in total commitments, reflecting strong support from both new and existing investors. The Fund attracted a diverse, global investor base, including pension funds, sovereign wealth funds, insurance companies, endowments and other institutional and wealth investors.

The Hybrid Value strategy focuses on delivering flexible, partnership-oriented solutions that sit between traditional debt and equity. The strategy primarily invests in structured equity opportunities, including preferred and convertible securities, and provides capital solutions to support growth initiatives, acquisitions, shareholder liquidity and balance sheet optimization, while seeking to provide downside protection and equity participation for investors.

We are grateful for the strong support from both new and existing investors in HVF III, which we believe reflects continued confidence in our strategy and track record, said Jason Scheir, Partner and Head of Hybrid Value at Apollo. We have built the Hybrid Value franchise to deliver bespoke, partnership capital at scale and we remain focused on generating attractive risk-adjusted returns for our investors.

HVF III follows Apollo Hybrid Value Fund I, which closed at $3.3 billion in 2019, and Apollo Hybrid Value Fund II, which closed at $4.6 billion in 2022. Building on this track record, Apollos broader hybrid ecosystem continues to expand with a growing base of capital across its hybrid strategies.

We believe hybrid strategies offer a compelling risk-reward framework for investors as they navigate market cycles and the current period of elevated uncertainty, said Matt Nord, Co-Head of Private Equity and Head of Hybrid at Apollo. Our ability to provide scaled, flexible capital, combined with the strength of our integrated platform, positions us to be the partner of choice for many of the worlds leading companies and sponsors.

Paul, Weiss, Rifkind, Wharton & Garrison LLP represented Apollo in connection with the closing of HVF III.

+ APOLLO GLOBAL MANAGEMENT APOLLO CLOSES ACCORD FUND VII AT $1.9 BILLION

Brings Total Capital Raised Across Accord Dislocation Complex to $11.6 Billion Since Inception

NEW YORK, May 04, 2026 -- Apollo (NYSE: APO) today announced the final close of Apollo Accord Fund VII (Accord VII or the Fund) with $1.9 billion in total commitments, reflecting broad support from a global and diverse group of investors including pension funds, financial institutions, endowments, foundations and family offices.

Accord VII is the latest vintage of the Firms flagship Accord Dislocation Series, which has raised $11.6 billion since inception in 2017. The strategy pursues dislocated liquid credit during periods of market volatility, as well as select idiosyncratic and issuer-driven opportunities in more stable market environments. The investment approach emphasizes a diversified portfolio of highly defensible positions targeting the top of the capital structure, across both primary and secondary markets.

We are operating in a period of heightened volatility driven by elevated valuations, increased geopolitical and macroeconomic risk and rapid AI-driven disruption, said Chris Lahoud, Partner and Deputy Co-Head of Hybrid at Apollo. In this environment, markets reward judgment, scale and disciplined underwriting. We believe periods of volatility and dispersion create compelling opportunities for capital providers who are prepared to act decisively.

Akila Grewal, Global Head of the Institutional Client Group at Apollo, added, We are grateful for the continued support of our investors globally. The strong demand for Accord VII reflects sustained confidence in the strategy and the important role it plays within diversified portfolios. In dynamic market environments, we believe the Funds flexible mandate can help investors take advantage of volatility with an emphasis on senior positioning within the capital structure.

Paul, Weiss, Rifkind, Wharton & Garrison LLP represented Apollo in connection with the closing of Accord VII.

+ QATAR AIRWAYS FURTHER REBUILDS FLIGHT SCHEDULES

Qatar Airways has published its latest revised schedule, reflecting the gradual increase in flights to, and from, Doha to more than 120 destinations by mid-May 2026, across the airline's global network. All flights to, and from, Doha continue to operate through dedicated flight corridors established in close co-ordination with the Qatar Civil Aviation Authority. Please note: Flight schedules are subject to change or cancellation due to operational, regulatory, safety, or other circumstances beyond our control. To view the schedule, click here. Passengers who currently hold a confirmed booking on a flight to the destinations listed in the new schedule will be contacted with new flight information. Please be sure to check the Qatar Airways website or app and ensure your contact details are correct and updated. Source: Company Website

+ 26 MARCH 2026: QATAR AIRWAYS CONTINUES TO RESTORE FLIGHT NETWORK

Qatar Airways has published its latest revised schedule, reflecting the gradual increase in flights to and from Doha. The latest update, valid until 15 April 2026, provides additional frequencies to more than 90 destinations across Qatar Airways' global network. All flights to and from Doha continue to operate through dedicated flight corridors. These routes are established in close coordination with the Qatar Civil Aviation Authority.

Passengers who currently hold a confirmed booking on a flight to one of the destinations listed in the new schedule will be contacted with new flight information. Please be sure to check the Qatar Airways website or app and ensure your contact details are correct and updated. Passengers are kindly asked not to arrive at their departure airport unless they hold a valid, confirmed ticket for travel. Please note: Flight schedules are subject to change or cancellation due to operational, regulatory, safety, or other circumstances beyond our control.

Our teams are doing everything within the current constraints to ensure our passengers reach their destinations. We apologise for any inconvenience caused during this period of disruption and thank passengers for their patience and understanding. Source: Company Website

+ QATAR AIRWAYS TO OPERATE LIMITED RELIEF CORRIDOR FROM DOHA

Qatar Airways scheduled flight operations remain temporarily suspended due to the closure of Qatari airspace. Qatar Airways will resume operations once the Qatar Civil Aviation Authority announces the safe full reopening of Qatari airspace by the relevant authorities. A further update will be provided on 8 March by 09:00 Doha time (06:00 UTC). Following temporary authorisation from the Qatar Civil Aviation Authority confirming a safe operating corridor, Qatar Airways intends to operate repatriation flights on 07 March, departing from Hamad International Airport to the following airports: London (LHR), Paris (CDG), Madrid (MAD), Rome (FCO) and Frankfurt (FRA). Priority on these flights was given to stranded passengers with families, elderly passengers, and those with urgent medical and compassionate travel needs. Each flight was pre-allocated by Qatar Airways directly to such affected passengers. These flights do not constitute a confirmation of resumption of scheduled commercial operations. Passengers are kindly requested not to proceed to the airport unless they have already received an official notification from Qatar Airways for these flights. Qatar Airways has contacted affected passengers directly with their assigned flight details, travel arrangements and next steps. We are working around the clock to organise additional relief flights where operationally possible and will share further updates as soon as they are confirmed. Source: Company Website

value.Title: American Securities Completes Sale Of CPM To Rosebank Industries

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJP-99B3-RRXR-P0N2-00000-00American Securities Completes Sale Of CPM To Rosebank Industries2026-05-12T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:56:12ZLexisNexisAmerican Securities Completes Sale Of CPM To Rosebank Industries

NEW YORK, May 12, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- American Securities LLC ("American Securities"), a leading U.S. private equity firm, today announced the completion of the sale of CPM Holdings, Inc. ("CPM"), a global leader in highly engineered process equipment and automation solutions, to Rosebank Industries plc (LON: ROSE) ("Rosebank"), in a transaction valued at approximately $2.1 billion.*
Headquartered in Blaine, Minnesota, CPM is an innovative leader in highly engineered processing equipment used in oilseed, animal feed production, renewable energy, plant-based foods and industrial materials. Since American Securities' investment in 2018, the company has strengthened its leadership position across key end markets while expanding its higher-margin aftermarket and recurring revenue offerings. CPM increased its presence in attractive growth areas, including renewable energy, and invested significantly in talent, technology, and operational capabilities to support long-term growth. The company also continued to drive innovation through initiatives such as Monarc, a proprietary technology designed to help customers produce clean label products more safely and efficiently at scale.


"We are incredibly proud of what the CPM team accomplished during our partnership," said Michael Sand, Partner at American Securities. "Together, we strengthened the company's operational capabilities, expanded into new markets, and advanced innovative technologies that position CPM for continued long-term growth. We thank Dave Webster and the entire CPM team and wish them continued success with Rosebank."
*Excludes potential earn-out payment based on the achievement of certain FY26 performance targets.
About American Securities
Founded in 1994, American Securities is a leading U.S. private equity firm that invests in North American companies, primarily in the industrial and B2B services sectors. With $23 billion under management, we partner with businesses generating $200 million to $2 billion in annual revenues. We combine deep sector expertise, differentiated insights and proven internal capabilities to serve as transformational partners that drive growth and build enduring value. Our investment philosophy emphasizes capital preservation through disciplined investing and hands-on engagement, paired with repeatable value creation processes and operational excellence. American Securities is based in New York with an office in Shanghai. For more information, please visit .
About CPM
Founded in 1883, CPM has evolved from its origins as the California Pellet Mill into a diversified industrial business. Headquartered in Blaine, Minnesota, the company has become an innovative leader in the design and manufacture of highly engineered process equipment and automation systems. CPM serves a global customer base, operating through three core segments: Industrial Solutions, Engineered Solutions and Process Solutions primarily serving the animal feed, oilseed processing, renewable energy, food, and other industrial markets. The company operates 35 facilities globally and maintains a commercial presence in more than 100 countries, with approximately 1,700 employees.

Prosek Partners

SOURCE American Securities LLC

value.Title: PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP - THE RECENT SECTOR UPDATES WITH PEERS

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJN-FNH3-S0MP-43V5-00000-00PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP - THE RECENT SECTOR UPDATES WITH PEERS2026-05-12T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:02:04ZLexisNexisSTOCKPILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP - THE RECENT SECTOR UPDATES WITH PEERS

DASHBOARD: PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP

PermID4296383614Websitewww.pillsburylaw.com/en/IndustryFull-Service BigLawAddress31 West 52nd Street, New York, NY 10019 USA

ACTIVITIES:

Pillsbury Winthrop Shaw Pittman LLP, founded in 1868, is a global law firm headquartered in New York City. With over 700 lawyers across 20 offices, it serves clients in energy, technology, and finance, specializing in M&A, regulatory, and litigation. Generating over $700 million annually, Pillsbury advised on 2024 renewable energy transactions, recognized by Columbia Law School. Its culture emphasizes innovation, diversity, and pro bono work, with strong associate training programs. Competing with Baker McKenzie, Pillsbury stands out for its energy and tech regulatory expertise. In 2024, it expanded its AI and ESG practices, focusing on sustainable finance. Recent initiatives include cloud-based legal tech and partnerships with nonprofits like Lawyers Alliance for New York, reinforcing its community impact.

+ PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP [1 News That Matters]

+ MORGAN LEWIS STRENGTHENS EMPLOYEE BENEFITS TEAM WITH PILLSBURY TALENT

Morgan Lewis & Bockius LLP has announced the addition of a prominent attorney from Pillsbury Winthrop Shaw Pittman LLP to its Employee Benefits team. This strategic move is seen as a response to the growing complexities in employee benefits law and the increasing demand for expert legal guidance in this area. The attorney, whose experience spans multiple facets of employee benefits, is expected to enhance Morgan Lewis’ capabilities in providing comprehensive legal services to its clients.The announcement comes as Morgan Lewis looks to bolster its offerings in employee benefits law, particularly in the wake of several recent regulatory changes. The attorney's arrival on 08th April 2026 is intended to strengthen the firm's position in an increasingly competitive legal landscape. Morgan Lewis has consistently focused on expanding its practice areas to meet client needs, and this latest addition is part of that ongoing strategy.Pillsbury Winthrop Shaw Pittman, known for its robust practice in employee benefits, has seen a number of its attorneys transition to other firms in recent years. The departing attorney brings a wealth of experience in compliance and regulatory issues, which will be invaluable to Morgan Lewis as it navigates the complexities of employee benefit plans and pension laws. This move not only reflects the attorney's individual career advancement but also highlights the fluid nature of talent within the legal sector.Morgan Lewis has a reputation for its deep expertise in various practice areas, and the inclusion of the Pillsbury attorney is expected to further enhance its service offerings. The firm has been proactive in adapting to changes in legislation affecting employee benefits, ensuring that its clients remain compliant and well-informed. By integrating new talent, Morgan Lewis aims to provide even more specialized services tailored to the evolving needs of its diverse client base.As the legal field continues to evolve, the strategic hiring of top talent from firms like Pillsbury is critical for maintaining a competitive edge. Morgan Lewis’ latest hiring signals its commitment to delivering high-quality legal solutions in employee benefits, reinforcing its reputation as a leading firm in this practice area. The legal community will be watching closely to see how this new addition impacts Morgan Lewis’ capabilities and client service in the months ahead.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ PERKINS COIE [3 News That Matters]

+ ASHURST PERKINS COIE MERGER GAINS STRONG SUPPORT FROM PARTNERS

The merger of global law firms Ashurst and Perkins Coie into the entity Ashurst Perkins Coie has received overwhelming support from the partnerships of both firms. This decision was finalized following a consultation period, with the approval announced on 13th April 2026. The merger is expected to close in the third quarter of the year, contingent upon customary closing conditions.Mark Birnbaum, executive committee chair of Perkins Coie, emphasized the significance of this approval, stating that it reflects the partners' commitment to creating a forward-looking firm. He noted that the merger underlines a shared confidence in their capacity to grow and deliver exceptional legal services in an evolving global market. Paul Jenkins, CEO of Ashurst, also highlighted the alignment between the two firms, asserting that their combined strengths will benefit their clients significantly.The merged firm aims to focus on key sectors including technology, energy, infrastructure, and financial services. Bill Malley, firmwide managing partner at Perkins Coie, expressed optimism that the combination would enhance the firms' abilities to meet clients’ complex cross-border legal needs. The new entity is set to boast approximately 3,000 lawyers across 52 offices in 32 countries, with major hubs located in Seattle, London, Sydney, and New York.The merger, first announced last year, brings together two firms with combined revenues of around USD 2.8 billion. Their lawyers have extensive experience in international markets, having worked in cities such as Brussels, Dubai, Frankfurt, Hong Kong, Paris, Seoul, Shenzhen, Singapore, and Tokyo. The leadership of the new firm will be shared by Malley and Jenkins as global co-CEOs, while Ashurst's global chair, Karen Davies, and Perkins Coie's corporate practice chair, Brian Eiting, will serve as co-chairs.As the legal landscape continues to evolve, the Ashurst Perkins Coie combination is poised to enhance its competitive edge by leveraging the strengths of both firms. This strategic move not only positions the new entity to better serve its clients but also sets the stage for sustained growth and performance in the legal industry.

+ PERKINS COIE AND ASHURST UNITE TO FORM ASHURST PERKINS COIE

NEW YORK — Perkins Coie and Ashurst announced today that the partnerships of both firms have voted overwhelmingly to approve their proposed combination. The approval follows consultation from both firms and reflects strong support for the combination of two law firms with highly complementary geographic reach, sector depth, and practice strength. The date of this announcement is recorded as 2026-04-14 16:18:02.950901.Upon closing, Ashurst Perkins Coie will emerge as a globally integrated firm with combined revenues of approximately US$2.8 billion. The transaction, announced on 1st May 2026, marks a pivotal moment for Esperion, which specializes in lipid management therapies.The deal is particularly noteworthy given the growing interest in private equity investments within the biotechnology space. By going private, Esperion aims to streamline its research and development processes and accelerate the commercialization of its innovative therapies. Law firms Sidley and Goodwin, recognized for their expertise in Mergers & Acquisitions, are expected to provide comprehensive support, ensuring a seamless transition during this transformative phase for the company. Their involvement also underscores the legal complexities that accompany such a high-stakes transaction.Esperion's decision to pursue a take-private strategy comes amid a broader trend of biotech firms seeking to enhance value through private ownership. Industry analysts suggest that this move could foster a more favorable environment for long-term strategic planning and investment in R&D. Investors are likely to monitor the performance of Esperion closely, as the company adjusts to its new status and seeks to enhance shareholder value post-transaction. The potential for accelerated growth in a less scrutinized environment presents a compelling narrative for both current and prospective investors.Sidley and Goodwin's participation in this deal illustrates their strong positioning within the private equity landscape, leveraging their extensive networks and legal acumen to facilitate complex transactions. The firms' reputations for excellence in handling high-profile deals will not only benefit Esperion but may also attract further opportunities in the life sciences sector. As the deal progresses, stakeholders will be keen to assess how the new private structure impacts Esperion's strategic initiatives and overall market performance.As Esperion embarks on this new path, the collaboration between Sidley and Goodwin in executing the $1.1 billion take-private deal will be closely watched by industry insiders and investors alike. This landmark transaction is expected to set a precedent for future deals within the biotech arena, highlighting the ongoing evolution of market dynamics in the face of advancing medical technology and shifting investor interests.

+ FBT GIBBONS WELCOMES GOODWIN PROCTER VENTURE CAPITAL ATTORNEY

FBT Gibbons has announced the addition of a venture capital attorney from Goodwin Procter LLP to its New Jersey office. This strategic hire comes as the firm aims to strengthen its capabilities in venture capital law, a sector that has seen significant growth in recent years.On 21st April 2026, FBT Gibbons confirmed the recruitment of the attorney, whose expertise in venture capital transactions is expected to complement the firm’s existing legal services. As the venture capital landscape continues to evolve, this move positions FBT Gibbons to better serve its clients in a competitive market.The attorney brings a wealth of experience from Goodwin Procter LLP, a recognized leader in the field, known for its robust venture capital practice that caters to startups and established companies alike. FBT Gibbons aims to leverage this expertise to enhance its offerings to clients seeking legal guidance in raising capital and navigating complex investment environments.This development is part of a broader trend within the legal industry, where firms are increasingly focusing on expanding their expertise in niche areas such as venture capital. FBT Gibbons' decision to hire from Goodwin Procter reflects its commitment to providing top-tier legal services that meet the evolving needs of clients in New Jersey and beyond.As FBT Gibbons continues to grow its team, industry observers will be keen to see how this addition impacts the firm’s standing in the competitive legal market. The firm’s strategic focus on venture capital could attract new clients and bolster its reputation as a leading provider of legal services in the region.

+ GOODWIN PROCTER COLLABORATES WITH TOPSPIN TO CLOSE $328M CONSUMER FUND

GoodwinProcter LLP has played a pivotal role in the successful closing of Topspin Consumer Partners' latest fundraising initiative, which has amassed a substantial $328 million. This new consumer-focused fund represents a significant development in the private equity landscape, with Goodwin Procter providing essential legal support throughout the fundraising process.On April 15th, 2026, Topspin announced the conclusion of its latest fund, which has been designed to capitalize on emerging opportunities within the consumer sector. The legal expertise from Goodwin Procter has been instrumental in navigating the complexities of fundraising, ensuring compliance with regulatory standards and streamlining the investment process for potential investors.The consumer-focused fund is expected to target a broad array of investment opportunities, emphasizing sectors that are experiencing robust growth. Topspin Consumer Partners, leveraging the guidance and support of Goodwin Procter, aims to identify and invest in innovative companies that are well-positioned to thrive in a rapidly evolving marketplace. This strategic focus aligns with current trends indicating a shift towards consumer-centric investments.Goodwin Procter’s involvement not only underscores its reputation as a leading law firm in private equity but also highlights its commitment to supporting clients in realizing their investment objectives. The firm’s adept handling of legal frameworks and partnership structures is expected to enhance the operational efficiency of Topspin’s new fund, thus improving investor confidence.As the market for consumer investments continues to expand, the collaboration between Goodwin Procter and Topspin is poised to set a precedent for future funds. Investors are keenly observing how this partnership will navigate the competitive landscape and deliver returns, with implications for the broader investment community.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ ORRICK, HERRINGTON & SUTCLIFFE LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ ORRICK WELCOMES SENIOR IP ATTORNEY FROM USPTO

Orrick, Herrington & Sutcliffe has strengthened its intellectual property (IP) practice by hiring a senior attorney from the U.S. Patent and Trademark Office (USPTO). This strategic addition is expected to enhance Orrick's capabilities in navigating complex patent issues, particularly as the firm continues to expand its footprint in the technology and telecommunications sectors.The new hire will officially join the firm on the 28th of April, 2026, bringing with them a wealth of experience from their tenure at the USPTO. With an extensive background in patent examination and litigation, the attorney's expertise aligns well with Orrick's focus on providing comprehensive legal solutions to its clients in rapidly evolving industries.Orrick's managing partner commented on the significance of this hire, noting that the firm's commitment to offering top-tier IP services is underscored by the addition of professionals who possess a deep understanding of the intricacies of patent law. This move is seen as part of Orrick's broader strategy to leverage the changing landscape of intellectual property rights in the digital age.The firm's IP practice has been gaining momentum in recent years, particularly in light of increasing demand for legal counsel in technology-driven sectors. By enhancing its team with experienced attorneys from key regulatory bodies like the USPTO, Orrick is well-positioned to meet the needs of clients facing complex challenges in patent law.As Orrick continues to attract talent from leading institutions, the legal community is keenly observing how these strategic hires will influence the firm's growth trajectory and its ability to provide innovative legal solutions. Investors and clients alike are likely to be interested in the potential impact of this development on Orrick's competitive standing in the market.

+ ORRICK WELCOMES YOUTH ONLINE SAFETY EXPERT NICOLE LOPEZ

Orrick, Herrington & Sutcliffe has announced the addition of Nicole Lopez, a leading authority in youth online safety policy and litigation, to its team. This strategic move comes at a crucial time as the firm aims to enhance its capabilities in navigating the evolving landscape of online safety. On 28th April 2026, Lopez expressed her enthusiasm for the new role and the opportunity to make a broader impact in the field.Lopez, who previously held a significant position at Meta, emphasized the importance of collaboration in her new role at Orrick. "I am so immensely proud of the pioneering work we did at Meta as a team, where I worked with incredibly talented colleagues who cared so deeply about getting it right," she stated. Her experience and expertise will be invaluable as Orrick seeks to provide comprehensive solutions for its clients facing increasing scrutiny over online safety issues.The urgency of youth online safety has escalated, with 25 state legislatures actively considering various forms of legislation aimed at safeguarding minors in digital environments. Jeremy Kudon, who leads Orrick’s State Legislative practice, noted, "We are facing a watershed moment. Online safety is top of mind for innovators, government, teens and parents, and society at large." Lopez's leadership in this conversation positions Orrick to effectively guide clients through the legislative maze.Aravind Swaminathan, Orrick’s Tech Sector Co-Leader, highlighted Lopez's multifaceted expertise, stating, "Nicole is part policy expert, part litigation and regulatory strategist, and part business advisor – she will be an extraordinary resource to our clients." As artificial intelligence continues to reshape the digital landscape, the need for proactive measures in youth safety is more pressing than ever, and Orrick is poised to be at the forefront of these changes.Orrick's commitment to developing robust online safety solutions is further exemplified by its Online Safety Law Center, which aims to keep clients informed of rapid developments in this area. With Nicole Lopez joining the team, the firm is well-positioned to advocate for effective policies and practices that balance innovation and child protection, ensuring that both companies and young users can thrive in a safer online environment.

+ ORRICK WELCOMES FORMER META YOUTH SAFETY CHIEF TO ITS TEAM

Orrick, Herrington & Sutcliffe has fortified its legal team with the appointment of the former head of youth safety policy at Meta, bringing notable expertise to the firm. This strategic hire aligns with Orrick's ongoing commitment to enhance its capabilities in digital security and compliance as the landscape of technology law continues to evolve.On the 23rd of April 2026, the firm announced this significant addition, underscoring its focus on safeguarding clients against emerging risks in the digital space. The former Meta executive, who has a robust background in policy formulation and implementation, is expected to contribute valuable insights into privacy and regulatory matters, particularly relating to the protection of young users in digital environments.The hiring of such a high-profile figure from Meta reflects Orrick's proactive approach in adapting to the shifting legal requirements surrounding youth safety online. As technology companies face increasing scrutiny over their practices, Orrick is positioning itself as a leader in providing comprehensive legal support tailored to the complexities of digital safety and compliance.Orrick's decision to engage with industry veterans like the former Meta youth safety policy head highlights the firm's strategy to not only keep pace with regulatory developments but also to anticipate the needs of its clients in a rapidly changing legal landscape. This move is likely to resonate well with clients who are navigating the multifaceted challenges of technology-related legal issues.As Orrick continues to expand its footprint in this crucial area of law, stakeholders are keenly observing how the integration of expertise from Meta will enhance the firm's offerings. The legal community is eager to see the impact of this new appointment on Orrick's ability to advise clients effectively on youth safety and digital compliance matters.

DASHBOARD: FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC

PermID4295901538Websitehttps://www.fortress.com/IndustryAlternative InvestmentsAddress1345 Avenue OF The Americas Florida 46 New York, NY, 10105-4302 United States

ACTIVITIES:

Fortress Investment Group LLC, founded in 1998, is a global investment firm headquartered in New York City. Employing over 900 people, it manages $48 billion in assets across private equity, credit, and real estate. Acquired by SoftBank in 2017, Fortress operates independently, focusing on distressed assets and infrastructure. In 2024, it invested $2 billion in renewable energy projects. Its culture emphasizes risk management, innovation, and agility, with training in asset valuation and fintech. Competing with Apollo Global, Fortress stands out for its credit expertise and opportunistic investments. Recent initiatives include blockchain-based real estate funds and ESG-focused portfolios, aligning with investment trends. Fortress diversified strategy ensures resilience in volatile markets.

+ FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC [3 Press Releases]

+ FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC MAMMOTH FREIGHTERS RECEIVES FAA CERTIFICATION FOR 777-200LRMF FREIGHTER

Fort Worth, Texas - April 8, 2026 - Mammoth Freighters LLC today announced it has received certification from the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) for its 777-200LRMF (Long Range Mammoth Freighter), marking a major milestone in the company's expansion of next-generation widebody freighter solutions.

FAA certification validates the aircraft's design, engineering, and performance, clearing the 777-200LRMF for commercial service. The platform delivers a compelling combination of long-range capability, payload efficiency, and operational reliability, positioning it as a highly versatile solution for global cargo networks.

Source: Company Website

+ BROOKFIELD ASSET MANAGEMENT ANNOUNCES PRICING OF $550 MILLION OF NOTES DUE 2031 AND $450 MILLION RE-OPENING OF NOTES DUE 2036

NEW YORK, April 14, 2026 -- Brookfield Asset Management Ltd. (BAM) (NYSE: BAM, TSX: BAM), a leading global alternative asset manager headquartered in New York with over $1 trillion of assets under management, today priced an offering of (i) $550 million principal amount of senior notes due 2031, which will bear interest at a rate of 4.832% per annum (the 2031 notes), and (ii) $450 million re-opening of its 5.298% notes due 2036 (the new 2036 notes and, together with the 2031 notes, the notes).

The new 2036 notes will form part of the same series as the already outstanding $400 million principal amount of 5.298% notes due 2036 (the existing 2036 notes), which were issued on November 18, 2025. After giving effect to the re-opening, the aggregate principal amount of the series will be $850 million. The terms of the new 2036 notes will be identical to the existing 2036 notes, other than the issue date and the issue price. The new 2036 notes will be issued at a price equal to 98.962% of their principal value plus accrued and unpaid interest from November 18, 2025 (the issue date of the existing 2036 notes) through, but excluding, the date of delivery of such notes, with an effective yield of 5.434%, if held to maturity.

The net proceeds from the sale of the notes will be used for general corporate purposes. The offering is expected to close on April 17, 2026, subject to the satisfaction of customary closing conditions.

The notes are being offered pursuant to prospectus supplements to BAMs existing base shelf prospectus filed in the United States and Canada and pursuant to an effective registration statement on Form F-10 on file with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission.

Copies of the prospectus supplement and accompanying base shelf prospectus may be obtained free of charge on EDGAR at www.sec.gov/edgar or on SEDAR+ at www.sedarplus.ca. + BROOKFIELD ASSET MANAGEMENT ANNOUNCES $1 BILLION COMMERCIAL PAPER PROGRAM

NEW YORK, March 03, 2026 -- Brookfield Asset Management Ltd. (NYSE: BAM) (TSX: BAM) (Brookfield) announced today that it has established a commercial paper program on a private placement basis. Under the program, Brookfield may issue unsecured commercial paper notes (the notes) up to a maximum aggregate amount outstanding at any time of $1 billion. The program is expected to further diversify and strengthen Brookfields balance sheet by providing another source of short-term capital. The proceeds from the issuance of any notes will be used for general corporate purposes.

The notes to be offered under the program have not been and will not be registered under the Securities Act of 1933, as amended (the Securities Act), and may not be offered or sold in the United States absent registration or an applicable exemption from registration requirements of the Securities Act and applicable state laws. This press release shall not constitute an offer to sell or the solicitation of an offer to buy notes under Brookfield Asset Managements commercial paper program and is being issued in accordance with Rule 135c under the Securities Act.

+ BROOKFIELD ASSET MANAGEMENT ANNOUNCES RENEWAL OF NORMAL COURSE ISSUER BID

NEW YORK, Jan. 09, 2026 -- Brookfield Asset Management Ltd. (NYSE: BAM, TSX: BAM) (Brookfield Asset Management) today announced it has received the requisite approval for the renewal of its normal course issuer bid providing the option to purchase up to 36,946,177 Class A Limited Voting Shares (Class A Shares), representing approximately 10% of the public float of Brookfield Asset Managements outstanding Class A Shares. Purchases under the bid will be made on the open market through the facilities of the New York Stock Exchange (NYSE), Toronto Stock Exchange (TSX), and/or alternative trading systems. The period of the normal course issuer bid will extend from January 13, 2026 to January 12, 2027, or an earlier date should Brookfield Asset Management complete its purchases. Brookfield Asset Management will pay the market price at the time of acquisition for any Class A Shares purchased or such other price as may be permitted.

As at December 31, 2025, the number of Class A Shares issued and outstanding totaled 1,637,941,906 of which 369,461,770 shares represented the public float. The maximum daily purchase on the TSX under this bid will be 365,499 Class A Shares, which is 25% of 1,461,999 (the average daily trading volume for Class A Shares on the TSX for the six months ended December 31, 2025).

Of the 37,123,295 Class A Shares approved for purchase under Brookfield Asset Managements prior normal course issuer bid that commenced on January 13, 2025 and will expire on January 12, 2026, Brookfield Asset Management purchased 6,548,561 Class A Shares as of December 31, 2025; 100,000 Class A Shares through open market purchases on the TSX and 6,448,561 Class A Shares through open market purchases on the NYSE and/or alternative trading systems. The weighted average price that Brookfield Asset Management paid per Class A Share acquired under this bid was US$54.14.

Brookfield Asset Management is renewing its normal course issuer bid to retain the option to acquire its Class A Shares where this aligns with its investment and capital allocation strategies. All Class A Shares acquired by Brookfield Asset Management under this bid will be cancelled and/or purchased by a non-independent trustee pursuant to the terms of Brookfield Asset Managements long-term incentive plans.

Brookfield Asset Management also announced that it has entered into an automatic purchase plan in relation to the normal course issuer bid. The automatic purchase plan allows for the potential purchase of Class A Shares during the term of the normal course issuer bid, subject to certain trading parameters, at times when Brookfield Asset Management ordinarily would not be active in the market due to its own internal trading black-out period, insider trading rules or otherwise. Outside of these periods, Class A Shares will be repurchased in accordance with managements discretion and in compliance with applicable law.

About Brookfield Asset Management

+ CARLYLE GROUP L.P. [3 Press Releases]

+ CARLYLE GROUP CENTERRA GOLD ANNOUNCES EXECUTIVE CHANGES

TORONTO, March 30, 2026 -- Centerra Gold Inc. (Centerra or the Company) (TSX: CG) (NYSE: CGAU) announces that David Hendriks, Executive Vice President and Chief Operating Officer (COO), will be leaving the Company. He will remain available to the Company in a consulting capacity to support a smooth transition. Mike Sylvestre will assume the responsibilities of COO on an interim basis, effective today.

President and CEO, Paul Tomory, commented, As we begin the search for a permanent replacement, we are pleased to welcome Mike Sylvestre as our interim Chief Operating Officer. Mike brings decades of operational leadership and deep technical expertise, and we are confident his experience and results-driven approach will support our sites as we remain focused on execution across our operating assets and continue to develop our organic growth pipeline of projects.

Mr. Sylvestre is a seasoned mining executive with over 45 years of international experience and has held leadership roles at major, mid-tier and junior mining companies. He most recently served as Senior Vice President, Americas at Kinross Gold, retiring in 2022. Over the course of his career, he has held a range of senior operational and executive roles, with a strong track record in leading both established and start-up operations, driving safety performance, operational excellence, and sustainable practices. He currently serves on the boards of Hochschild Mining and Vista Gold Corp. Mr. Sylvestre holds a Master of Science in Mining and Mineral Engineering from McGill University and a Bachelor of Science in Mining Engineering from Queens University and is a member of the Professional Engineers of Ontario.

About Centerra Gold

Centerra Gold Inc. is a Canadian-based gold mining company focused on operating, developing, exploring and acquiring gold and copper properties in North America, Turkiye, and other markets worldwide. Centerra operates two mines: the Mount Milligan Mine in British Columbia, Canada, and the Oksut Mine in Turkiye. The Company also owns the Kemess Project in British Columbia, Canada, the Goldfield Project in Nevada, United States, and owns and operates the Molybdenum Business Unit in the United States and Canada. Centerras shares trade on the Toronto Stock Exchange (TSX) under the symbol CG and on the New York Stock Exchange (NYSE) under the symbol CGAU. The Company is based in Toronto, Ontario, Canada.

+ APOLLO GLOBAL MANAGEMENT APOLLO HYBRID VALUE FUND III RAISES $6.5 BILLION

NEW YORK, May 05, 2026 -- Apollo (NYSE: APO) today announced the final close of Apollo Hybrid Value Fund III (HVF III), raising approximately $6.5 billion in total commitments, reflecting strong support from both new and existing investors. The Fund attracted a diverse, global investor base, including pension funds, sovereign wealth funds, insurance companies, endowments and other institutional and wealth investors.

The Hybrid Value strategy focuses on delivering flexible, partnership-oriented solutions that sit between traditional debt and equity. The strategy primarily invests in structured equity opportunities, including preferred and convertible securities, and provides capital solutions to support growth initiatives, acquisitions, shareholder liquidity and balance sheet optimization, while seeking to provide downside protection and equity participation for investors.

We are grateful for the strong support from both new and existing investors in HVF III, which we believe reflects continued confidence in our strategy and track record, said Jason Scheir, Partner and Head of Hybrid Value at Apollo. We have built the Hybrid Value franchise to deliver bespoke, partnership capital at scale and we remain focused on generating attractive risk-adjusted returns for our investors.

HVF III follows Apollo Hybrid Value Fund I, which closed at $3.3 billion in 2019, and Apollo Hybrid Value Fund II, which closed at $4.6 billion in 2022. Building on this track record, Apollos broader hybrid ecosystem continues to expand with a growing base of capital across its hybrid strategies.

We believe hybrid strategies offer a compelling risk-reward framework for investors as they navigate market cycles and the current period of elevated uncertainty, said Matt Nord, Co-Head of Private Equity and Head of Hybrid at Apollo. Our ability to provide scaled, flexible capital, combined with the strength of our integrated platform, positions us to be the partner of choice for many of the worlds leading companies and sponsors.

Paul, Weiss, Rifkind, Wharton & Garrison LLP represented Apollo in connection with the closing of HVF III.

+ APOLLO GLOBAL MANAGEMENT APOLLO CLOSES ACCORD FUND VII AT $1.9 BILLION

Brings Total Capital Raised Across Accord Dislocation Complex to $11.6 Billion Since Inception

NEW YORK, May 04, 2026 -- Apollo (NYSE: APO) today announced the final close of Apollo Accord Fund VII (Accord VII or the Fund) with $1.9 billion in total commitments, reflecting broad support from a global and diverse group of investors including pension funds, financial institutions, endowments, foundations and family offices.

Accord VII is the latest vintage of the Firms flagship Accord Dislocation Series, which has raised $11.6 billion since inception in 2017. The strategy pursues dislocated liquid credit during periods of market volatility, as well as select idiosyncratic and issuer-driven opportunities in more stable market environments. The investment approach emphasizes a diversified portfolio of highly defensible positions targeting the top of the capital structure, across both primary and secondary markets.

We are operating in a period of heightened volatility driven by elevated valuations, increased geopolitical and macroeconomic risk and rapid AI-driven disruption, said Chris Lahoud, Partner and Deputy Co-Head of Hybrid at Apollo. In this environment, markets reward judgment, scale and disciplined underwriting. We believe periods of volatility and dispersion create compelling opportunities for capital providers who are prepared to act decisively.

Akila Grewal, Global Head of the Institutional Client Group at Apollo, added, We are grateful for the continued support of our investors globally. The strong demand for Accord VII reflects sustained confidence in the strategy and the important role it plays within diversified portfolios. In dynamic market environments, we believe the Funds flexible mandate can help investors take advantage of volatility with an emphasis on senior positioning within the capital structure.

Paul, Weiss, Rifkind, Wharton & Garrison LLP represented Apollo in connection with the closing of Accord VII.

+ APOLLO FUNDS ACQUIRE GATEHOUSE LIVING GROUP FROM GATEHOUSE BANK

Apollo (NYSE: APO) today announced that Apollo-managed funds have acquired Gatehouse Living Group ("GLG" or "the Group"), a vertically integrated UK residential investment and management platform, from Gatehouse Bank ("GHB"). Financial terms of the transaction were not disclosed.

The Group is comprised of its investment arm, Gatehouse Investment Management ("GIM"), as well as its property management arm, Ascend Properties ("Ascend"). Together, the Group provides institutional investors with a comprehensive suite of operational services to originate and manage new build-to-rent single family housing. As a leader in the UK, the Group has established five platforms and successfully exited three, acquiring more than 5,000 homes from the UK's largest housebuilders. Through Ascend, the Group manages more than 10,000 homes nationwide for both itself and third-party clients, nearly 4,000 of which are operated under the resident-facing white-label Ascend Living brand.

"GLG has led the growth of institutional rental housing, creating a prominent platform that strategically supports the sector and accelerates the delivery of much-needed high-quality housing throughout the UK," said Paul Stockwell, Chief Executive Officer of GLG. "This transaction with Apollo is a testament to the strength of our holistic investment and property management platform and will support our continued expansion in the market."

"GLG, under Gatehouse Bank's stewardship, has established itself as a vertically integrated leader in the UK housing sector, supporting the supply of high-quality, professionally managed rental homes," said Edward Jones, Partner at Apollo. "We look forward to working closely with management to further enhance and expand its operational capabilities and to supporting the platform's continued success as the UK focuses on bolstering homebuilding across tenures and attracting more investment to the sector."

"We are incredibly proud of the success achieved since GLG launched its first investment in 2014 and the critical role we have played, as an early entrant, in establishing the UK Build to Rent market," said Charles Haresnape, Chief Executive Officer of GHB. "The sale is part of Gatehouse Bank's long-term retail growth strategy and will allow for further development of its home finance product offering, which has seen strong demand supporting UK resident, UK expat and international homebuyers and landlords. We are confident that GLG is well positioned today to continuing building on its strong foundation, supporting the supply of new rental homes in the UK."

GLG will continue to be led by CEO Paul Stockwell and its management team, and it will be rebranded with an announcement in due course. The Group will invest its own capital and that of third-party partners, and Ascend will maintain its property management services for external institutional portfolios.

All flights to and from Doha continue to operate through dedicated flight corridors. These routes are established in close coordination with the Qatar Civil Aviation Authority.

+ QATAR AIRWAYS TO OPERATE LIMITED RELIEF CORRIDOR FROM DOHA

Qatar Airways scheduled flight operations remain temporarily suspended due to the closure of Qatari airspace. Qatar Airways will resume operations once the Qatar Civil Aviation Authority announces the safe full reopening of Qatari airspace by the relevant authorities. A further update will be provided on 8 March by 09:00 Doha time (06:00 UTC). Following temporary authorisation from the Qatar Civil Aviation Authority confirming a safe operating corridor, Qatar Airways intends to operate repatriation flights on 07 March, departing from Hamad International Airport to the following airports: London (LHR), Paris (CDG), Madrid (MAD), Rome (FCO) and Frankfurt (FRA). Priority on these flights was given to stranded passengers with families, elderly passengers, and those with urgent medical and compassionate travel needs. Each flight was pre-allocated by Qatar Airways directly to such affected passengers. These flights do not constitute a confirmation of resumption of scheduled commercial operations. Passengers are kindly requested not to proceed to the airport unless they have already received an official notification from Qatar Airways for these flights. Qatar Airways has contacted affected passengers directly with their assigned flight details, travel arrangements and next steps. We are working around the clock to organise additional relief flights where operationally possible and will share further updates as soon as they are confirmed. Source: Company Website

urn:contentItem:6JJN-SVX3-S0MR-H47V-00000-00ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION - THE RECENT SECTOR UPDATES WITH PEERS2026-05-12T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:02:04ZLexisNexisSTOCKROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION - THE RECENT SECTOR UPDATES WITH PEERS

DASHBOARD: ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION

PermID4296528943Websitehttps://www.rockefellerfoundation.org/IndustryPhilanthropy / Global DevelopmentAddress420 Fifth Avenue, New York City, New York, U.S.

ACTIVITIES:

The Rockefeller Foundation, founded in 1913 by John D. Rockefeller, is a global philanthropy headquartered in New York City. With around 200 staff, it manages a $6 billion endowment, granting $300 million annually to advance health, equity, and sustainability. In 2024, it committed $100 million to climate resilience in African cities. Its culture emphasizes innovation, collaboration, and data-driven impact, with training in global policy and grantmaking. Competing with Ford Foundation, Rockefeller stands out for its pioneering role in public health and green energy, including founding the Global Energy Alliance for People and Planet. Recent initiatives include AI for social good and support for the National Museum of African American History, aligning with social justice trends. The foundations century-long legacy includes eradicating hookworm and funding the Green Revolution, shaping modern philanthropy.

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION [3 News That Matters]

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION MARKS 60 YEARS IN NAIROBI, PAVING NEW PATHS IN AFRICAN DEVELOPMENT

NAIROBI — The Rockefeller Foundation celebrated the 60th anniversary of its Africa Regional Office, established in Nairobi, Kenya, in 1966, on the 1st of May, 2026. This milestone event was part of the AfricaXchange 2026, which aims to catalyze systemic reform by uniting leaders who can deploy capital, influence policy discussions, and scale viable solutions to the continent's pressing challenges. The Rockefeller Foundation has a rich legacy of over a century of investing in human capital, health, agriculture, and energy across Africa, and this event brought together more than 400 leaders from various sectors to discuss collaborative solutions and advocate for systemic change.William Asiko, Senior Vice President and head of The Rockefeller Foundation’s Africa Regional Office, emphasized the changing landscape of development finance during his opening remarks. "African philanthropy, private capital, and regional institutions are reshaping not just how development is financed, but how it is delivered," he stated. His remarks come at a time when the Organization for Economic Co-operation and Development (OECD) reported a significant decline in official development assistance, highlighting the urgent need for new models of investment and partnership in Africa. The AfricaXchange event sought to position Africa not merely as a recipient of aid but as an equal partner in shaping its economic destiny.The closing session featured Elizabeth Yee, Executive Vice President of Programs at The Rockefeller Foundation, who remarked on the importance of collective action. "Philanthropy’s job is not to be the hero. It’s to take risk others can’t, build trust where it’s been missing, and move countries from ambition to execution," she noted. The conference's theme, "Money, Markets, and Mindsets," captured the ambition of ensuring that Africa's future is built, financed, and led by Africans. The Rockefeller Foundation also launched the inaugural class of Africa Big Bets Fellows, ten changemakers driving community-led solutions across several African countries.AfricaXchange 2026 served as a platform for discussions centered on activating capital and policy levers necessary for self-determination. Leaders from diverse fields tackled urgent issues such as energy transition, climate-resilient agriculture, and health systems transformation. Among the distinguished participants were E. Prof. Anyang’ Nyong’o, Governor of Kisumu County, and E. Nardos Bekele-Thomas, CEO of the African Union Development Agency. Their presence underscored the collaborative spirit needed to address the continent's challenges while fostering sustainable growth and resilience.The Rockefeller Foundation's commitment to Africa began long before the Nairobi office opened, with initiatives dating back to the early 20th century, including the establishment of the West Africa Yellow Fever Commission in 1925. Today, the Foundation dedicates approximately one-third of its funding to Africa, priori

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION LAUNCHES BIG BETS FELLOWSHIP TO TRANSFORM AFRICA

The Rockefeller Foundation has launched the inaugural class of Africa Big Bets Fellows during its AfricaXchange convening in Nairobi, Kenya. The first cohort includes 10 changemakers from Ghana, Kenya, Malawi, Nigeria, South Africa, and Tanzania, selected to lead innovative, community-driven solutions addressing key development challenges across the continent. The fellowship will run for five months and aims to help scale impactful ideas in areas such as energy access, food security, healthcare, financial inclusion, climate resilience, and migration.This initiative coincides with the 28th of April, 2026, marking the 60th anniversary of the Rockefeller Foundation’s Africa Regional Office and reflects its long-standing engagement in the continent’s development. Leaders from the Foundation emphasized that the fellowship supports a new generation of African innovators whose locally driven solutions are designed to expand opportunity, strengthen resilience, and improve quality of life across diverse sectors including health, agriculture, clean energy, and digital finance.Across Africa, the program comes at a time when major structural challenges persist, including limited access to electricity for hundreds of millions of people and significant gaps in global climate finance. Despite these challenges, Africa holds vast potential for renewable energy development and sustainable growth, with projections indicating that a large share of future energy demand could be met through clean energy sources if adequately supported.The selected fellows are working on a wide range of solutions, including mobile healthcare services in rural Ghana, waste-to-resource systems for cleaner cities, regenerative agriculture linked to school feeding programs in Kenya, AI-powered farming innovation in Malawi, and improved access to financial services in Nigeria. Other projects focus on clean energy access for off-grid communities, climate-resilient farming, youth-led media platforms, and real-time migration and climate information systems.The fellowship is part of a broader AfricaXchange platform that brings together funders, practitioners, and policymakers to strengthen locally led development. It reflects a growing emphasis on ensuring that African solutions are designed, financed, and led within the continent, while also connecting innovators to global networks and resources to scale their impact. The Rockefeller Foundation highlighted that this initiative builds on more than a century of work in Africa, continuing its legacy by supporting emerging leaders whose ideas aim to deliver measurable, scalable, and sustainable impact across the continent and beyond.

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION INVESTS IN WORKFORCE RESILIENCE AMID AI CHALLENGES

The Rockefeller Foundation is taking a proactive stance against the impending challenges posed by artificial intelligence on the workforce, committing significant funding to a national initiative aimed at enhancing workforce resilience. On 28th April 2026, the century-old philanthropic institution announced its strategy to address the disruption caused by automation, focusing on preventing job displacement and helping workers transition into AI-complementary roles.The initiative is designed as a grant-based program that prioritizes workforce interventions over product development. The Rockefeller Foundation's approach emphasizes the importance of program design, metrics, and evaluation. Central to this effort will be the measurement of displacement risk, deployment outcomes, and labor-market transitions, which are crucial for understanding the impact of automation on employment.Funding for the initiative is expected to come through various instruments, including grants, programmatic pilots, and partnerships with employers, training providers, and local governments. The choices made during the implementation phase will be critical in determining whether the Rockefeller Foundation's funding leads to scalable reskilling pipelines or merely funds isolated projects with limited long-term impact.This move by the Rockefeller Foundation serves as a strategic philanthropic signal rather than a regulatory or corporate initiative. By investing at this scale, the foundation aims to catalyze innovative models for retraining and public-private collaboration, complementing ongoing discussions about workforce policies such as universal basic income, sectoral training, and apprenticeship programs. This initiative is poised to increase the demand for measurable, data-driven evaluations within the skills and labor analytics ecosystem.As the Rockefeller Foundation rolls out this substantial program, stakeholders will be keenly observing its funding priorities, evaluation criteria, and partnership dynamics. Key considerations will include whether the initiative emphasizes durable credentialing, employer engagement, or short-term placement metrics. Ultimately, the decisions made now will be pivotal in determining whether this initiative can effectively influence labor-market outcomes or become primarily a source of funding for pilot programs.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ FORD FOUNDATION [3 News That Matters]

+ FORD FOUNDATION UNVEILS $10M HOMETOWN FUND TO REVITALIZE ROSEBURG

ROSEBURG, Ore. – The Ford Foundation is launching the Hometown Fund, a $10 million investment aimed at revitalizing Roseburg over the next decade. The date of this announcement is 5th May 2026. This initiative is designed to support large-scale, community-driven capital projects within the city limits, with the foundation offering capital grants of up to $1 million and pre-development planning requests of up to $75,000. The Ford Foundation aims to unite partners from both the private and public sectors to create impactful transformations in the community."So I really, really hope that the grown-ups can work together to build tangible spaces and places where kids can say that they are loved and that there’s a future here," stated Kara Inae Carlisle, President and CEO of the Ford Foundation. She emphasized the importance of creating meaningful changes in the community over the next decade, reflecting a commitment to fostering environments where families can thrive. This local initiative builds upon the foundation's extensive legacy of charitable giving, which has amounted to approximately $810 million since its inception.Historically, the Ford Foundation has focused on education, early childhood development, and community economic development. Its flagship Ford Scholars Program currently supports around 1,000 students annually, providing them with the necessary resources to pursue their academic goals. The foundation has also played a crucial role in regional crisis recovery and long-term community building, such as its partnership with the Oregon Community Foundation and Meyer Memorial Trust to distribute $10 million for community rebuilding following the devastating 2020 Labor Day wildfires.Organizations interested in applying for the Hometown Fund must start by completing an online information form that outlines their project’s scope and its expected impact on the community. After the initial submission, staff from the Ford Foundation will review the proposal and reach out to schedule a meeting with the applicant. Projects that align with the fund’s objectives of fostering multi-sector collaboration and large-scale transformation will receive an invitation to complete a formal application.The Hometown Fund represents a significant commitment by the Ford Foundation to invest in the future of Roseburg, aiming to create lasting change that benefits both current residents and future generations. As the foundation seeks to unite various stakeholders across sectors, the initiative reflects a broader vision for community revitalization in rural areas.

+ FORD FOUNDATION RECOGNIZED BY NHRC FOR COMMITMENT TO HUMAN RIGHTS IN NIGERIA

The National Human Rights Commission (NHRC) has honored the Ford Foundation for its sustained contributions to advancing human rights in Nigeria, reinforcing a partnership that has played a critical role in strengthening access to justice and institutional capacity. On 28th April 2026, during a ceremony in Abuja, the NHRC presented an Award of Appreciation to the Foundation in recognition of its longstanding support.The award was received by the Foundation’s Regional Director for West Africa, ChiChi Aniagolu-Okoye, during a high-level engagement with directors and senior officials of the Commission. Speaking at the event, the Executive Secretary of the NHRC, Dr. Tony Ojukwu, commended the Ford Foundation’s consistent backing, particularly in initiatives aimed at expanding access to justice and strengthening the Commission’s operational capacity.Dr. Ojukwu emphasized that given the breadth and complexity of human rights challenges in Nigeria, the Commission cannot function in isolation. He stated that sustained collaboration with development partners remains essential to transforming human rights protection from policy intentions into tangible outcomes for citizens.In her remarks, Aniagolu-Okoye described the recognition as both significant and encouraging, reaffirming the Foundation’s commitment to promoting human rights. She noted that a key focus of the Foundation’s work is ensuring that citizens are not only aware of their rights but are also supported in seeking justice when those rights are violated. However, she emphasized that awareness alone is insufficient without strong and effective enforcement systems.The engagement underscored the importance of continued cooperation between the Commission and its partners in addressing evolving human rights challenges across the country. In a vote of thanks, Senior Human Rights Adviser to the Commission, Hilary Ogbonna, expressed appreciation for the Ford Foundation’s enduring partnership, highlighting key institutional advancements, including initiatives such as the MOVE project and the Commission’s dashboard system, which benefitted from the Foundation’s early support.

+ FORD FOUNDATION URGES ECOWAS FIRST LADIES TO ADDRESS GENDER-BASED VIOLENCE

The Ford Foundation has called on First Ladies across the ECOWAS region to deploy their influence more strategically in tackling gender-based violence (GBV). The Foundation described the First Ladies’ 'soft power' as a critical driver of social change and institutional accountability. This call was made by ChiChi Aniagolu, West Africa Regional Director of the Ford Foundation, while delivering a goodwill message at the ECOWAS First Ladies’ Forum on Zero Tolerance for Gender-Based Violence, held in Banjul under the SWEDD+ platform in partnership with the World Bank and the United Nations Population Fund on 24th April 2026.Aniagolu warned that despite existing frameworks and commitments, gender-based violence remains widespread, with one in three women globally affected. In West and Central Africa, she noted, harmful practices such as child marriage persist, with over 30 percent of girls in some countries married before the age of 18. She also highlighted the economic burden of GBV, estimating losses of up to 3.7 percent of Gross Domestic Product in affected countries — figures that exceed investments in key sectors such as education.While the Ford Foundation acknowledged that First Ladies are not formal policymakers, it stressed their significant role in shaping public discourse and influencing decision-making across the continent. Aniagolu cited examples of impactful First Ladies, including Maryam Babangida, whose Better Life for Rural Women Programme elevated rural women's issues into national discussions, and Fatima Bio, whose 'Hands Off Our Girls' campaign contributed to a national emergency declaration on rape in 2019. Such instances illustrate how 'soft power,' when utilized effectively, can instigate systemic change.The Ford Foundation has continued to invest in strengthening GBV prevention and response systems across West Africa, including partnerships involving First Ladies and governors’ spouses in Nigeria. These interventions have aimed to advance advocacy efforts, improve evidence-based response systems, and address harmful social norms. Aniagolu emphasized that laws and policies are insufficient without public trust and accessibility, pointing out the persistent silence among survivors driven by stigma, fear, and weak institutional confidence.In light of recent regional initiatives such as the GBV Roadmap (2026–2028), Legal Scorecard, and Joint Communiqu , Aniagolu urged First Ladies to move beyond mere commitments by promoting safe reporting, challenging harmful norms, and strengthening accountability systems. 'No girl should grow up believing silence is her only option, and no woman should be denied justice because of where she lives,' she asserted, reaffirming the Ford Foundation’s commitment to supporting advocacy, strengthening systems, and amplifying women’s voices across the region.

value.Title: American Securities Completes Sale of CPM to Rosebank Industries

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJW-4143-RV0J-R2SN-00000-00American Securities Completes Sale of CPM to Rosebank Industries2026-05-12T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:56:12ZLexisNexisAmerican Securities Completes Sale of CPM to Rosebank Industries

Key Highlights:

* CPM strengthens leadership in key end markets and expands aftermarket and recurring revenue offerings.

* Company increases presence in renewable energy and invests in talent, technology, and operational capabilities.

* CPM drives innovation through proprietary technology Monarc for clean label product production.

Original Press Release:

May 12 -- American Securities LLC issued the following news release:

American Securities LLC ("American Securities"), a leading U.S. private equity firm, today announced the completion of the sale of CPM Holdings, Inc. ("CPM"), a global leader in highly engineered process equipment and automation solutions, to Rosebank Industries plc (LON: ROSE) ("Rosebank"), in a transaction valued at approximately $2.1 billion.[*]

Headquartered in Blaine, Minnesota, CPM is an innovative leader in highly engineered processing equipment used in oilseed, animal feed production, renewable energy, plant-based foods and industrial materials. Since American Securities' investment in 2018, the company has strengthened its leadership position across key end markets while expanding its higher-margin aftermarket and recurring revenue offerings. CPM increased its presence in attractive growth areas, including renewable energy, and invested significantly in talent, technology, and operational capabilities to support long-term growth. The company also continued to drive innovation through initiatives such as Monarc, a proprietary technology designed to help customers produce clean label products more safely and efficiently at scale.

"We are incredibly proud of what the CPM team accomplished during our partnership," said Michael Sand, Partner at American Securities. "Together, we strengthened the company's operational capabilities, expanded into new markets, and advanced innovative technologies that position CPM for continued long-term growth. We thank Dave Webster and the entire CPM team and wish them continued success with Rosebank."

About American Securities

Founded in 1994, American Securities is a leading U.S. private equity firm that invests in North American companies, primarily in the industrial and B2B services sectors. With $23 billion under management, we partner with businesses generating $200 million to $2 billion in annual revenues. We combine deep sector expertise, differentiated insights and proven internal capabilities to serve as transformational partners that drive growth and build enduring value. Our investment philosophy emphasizes capital preservation through disciplined investing and hands-on engagement, paired with repeatable value creation processes and operational excellence. American Securities is based in New York with an office in Shanghai. For more information, please visit www.american-securities.com.

About CPM

Founded in 1883, CPM has evolved from its origins as the California Pellet Mill into a diversified industrial business. Headquartered in Blaine, Minnesota, the company has become an innovative leader in the design and manufacture of highly engineered process equipment and automation systems. CPM serves a global customer base, operating through three core segments: Industrial Solutions, Engineered Solutions and Process Solutions primarily serving the animal feed, oilseed processing, renewable energy, food, and other industrial markets. The company operates 35 facilities globally and maintains a commercial presence in more than 100 countries, with approximately 1,700 employees.

Footnote:

[*] Excludes potential earn-out payment based on the achievement of certain FY26 performance targets.

[Category: Private Equity and Venture Capital, Investments, Mergers and Acquisitions]

Source: American Securities LLC

urn:contentItem:6JJG-MN03-RXCM-301N-00000-00FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC - INDUSTRY AND PEER INSIGHTS: RECENT TRENDS AND KEY HIGHLIGHTS2026-05-11T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:02:04ZLexisNexisSTOCKFORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC - INDUSTRY AND PEER INSIGHTS: RECENT TRENDS AND KEY HIGHLIGHTS

DASHBOARD: FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC

PermID4295901538Websitehttps://www.fortress.com/IndustryAlternative InvestmentsAddress1345 Avenue OF The Americas Florida 46 New York, NY, 10105-4302 United States

ACTIVITIES:

Fortress Investment Group LLC, founded in 1998, is a global investment firm headquartered in New York City. Employing over 900 people, it manages $48 billion in assets across private equity, credit, and real estate. Acquired by SoftBank in 2017, Fortress operates independently, focusing on distressed assets and infrastructure. In 2024, it invested $2 billion in renewable energy projects. Its culture emphasizes risk management, innovation, and agility, with training in asset valuation and fintech. Competing with Apollo Global, Fortress stands out for its credit expertise and opportunistic investments. Recent initiatives include blockchain-based real estate funds and ESG-focused portfolios, aligning with investment trends. Fortress diversified strategy ensures resilience in volatile markets.

+ FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC [3 Press Releases]

+ FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC MAMMOTH FREIGHTERS RECEIVES FAA CERTIFICATION FOR 777-200LRMF FREIGHTER

Fort Worth, Texas - April 8, 2026 - Mammoth Freighters LLC today announced it has received certification from the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) for its 777-200LRMF (Long Range Mammoth Freighter), marking a major milestone in the company's expansion of next-generation widebody freighter solutions.

FAA certification validates the aircraft's design, engineering, and performance, clearing the 777-200LRMF for commercial service. The platform delivers a compelling combination of long-range capability, payload efficiency, and operational reliability, positioning it as a highly versatile solution for global cargo networks.

Source: Company Website
